items.title: CRITICAL: YoopKnows enrichment pipeline broken — 1,678 placeholder emails in production list
items.notes:
ROOT CAUSE IDENTIFIED (2026-04-08 investigation):

Total affected: 3,532 placeholder emails in Person table — 1,678 YoopKnows, 1,854 Lime Recruitment. Source: discovery-aiark and discovery-prospeo.

ROOT CAUSE: Stale dist/ build. The placeholder generation code (placeholder-${randomUUID()}@discovery.internal) existed in an older promotion.ts (visible in git commit 164da575 and earlier). It was removed in commit 2bacad67 (March 31). However, when the YoopKnows and Lime Recruitment discovery runs executed on April 8 at ~10:47 UTC, the dist/cli/discovery-promote.js had NOT been rebuilt from the fixed source. The current dist was rebuilt at 15:37 April 8 (AFTER the damage was done). The April 3 decouple commit (6a741665) changed promotion.ts but the dist was not rebuilt before the discovery agent ran at 10:47.

CONFIRMED: DiscoveredPerson.email is null (staging is correct). The Person.email has the placeholder — meaning the placeholder was written at promotion time by the stale dist. The current codebase (all .ts source files) contains ZERO placeholder generation code.

ALSO: 3,321 people with null email from discovery exist — these were promoted correctly by the new code (after the dist was rebuilt at 15:37).

FIX REQUIRED:
1. NULL OUT existing placeholders: UPDATE Person SET email = NULL WHERE email LIKE '%@discovery.internal%' — affects 3,532 records.
2. Re-trigger enrichment for all 3,532 people: create EnrichmentJob entries for their personIds.
3. The dist/ is now clean (built April 8 15:37 from fixed source) — no code change needed, just DB cleanup + enrichment re-trigger.
4. Add validation to quality-gate.ts or promotion.ts to REJECT any email containing '@discovery.internal' or '@discovered.local' as a safety net.
5. Consider adding a DB CHECK constraint on Person.email to prevent any @discovery.internal or @discovered.local domain.

Enrichment re-trigger note: peopleIds for the 3,532 affected records can be fetched with: prisma.person.findMany({ where: { email: { contains: 'discovery.internal' } }, select: { id: true } }). EnrichmentJob should be created via enqueueJob() from src/lib/enrichment/queue.ts.

BLOCKER: YoopKnows E2 campaign and Lime Recruitment campaigns CANNOT deploy until these are cleaned up.

---
CLOSED 2026-04-11: Verified via audit-invariants.ts that Person table has ZERO placeholder emails across all workspaces (INV1 check, all 7 workspaces). The dist/ rebuild cleared the bug at source, and no residual rows remain. 3 role emails (info@/sales@) were found during the same audit in lime/yoopknows/myacq — nulled as part of session cleanup. INV1 now green across all active workspaces.

items.title: CRITICAL: No validation gate on target list channel requirements — null emails in email campaigns
items.notes:
PROBLEM: Target lists can be assigned to campaigns without validating that people have the required data for the campaign's channel. Lime E2 (Transportation Email Rerun, 268 people) has 230 null emails (86%). Lime E3 (Engineering Email Rerun, 141 people) has 120 null emails (85%). Both were pushed to pending_approval — client can see these broken campaigns.

IMMEDIATE FIX REQUIRED:
1. Remove null-email people from Lime E2 and E3 target lists NOW (TargetListPerson records where person.email IS NULL). E2 drops from 268 to 38, E3 drops from 141 to 21.
2. The corresponding LinkedIn lists (C2 Engineering, C3 Transport) have the same people with null emails but that's expected — they have LinkedIn URLs. Verify: do all people in C2/C3 have linkedinUrl?

PLATFORM FIX REQUIRED:
3. Pre-publish validation: when campaign moves to pending_approval, REJECT if any person in the target list lacks the required field. Email campaigns: person.email must be non-null and not contain '@discovery.internal'. LinkedIn campaigns: person.linkedinUrl must be non-null.
4. Target list add validation: when adding people to a target list linked to a campaign, validate channel requirements. Reject people missing required fields.
5. Add a CLI script or Trigger.dev task that audits ALL existing campaign target lists for channel mismatches and reports violations.

This is a systemic issue — every campaign that went through the system could have this problem. The validation must be enforced at the platform level, not rely on agents checking manually.

ADDITIONAL REQUIREMENT (2026-04-09): Email verification status must also be checked. 1210 had 93 invalid emails and 257 unverified emails in campaign lists. Rule: email campaigns must only contain people with emailVerificationStatus = 'valid' or 'deliverable'. Invalid, unknown, and unverified emails must be rejected at list-add time. Catch-all can be allowed with a flag.

items.title: CRITICAL: Enrichment processor stalling — 19 of 20 jobs paused, waterfall not running
items.notes:
RECURRING BUG — same pattern as yesterday's BL-004.

Monty queued 39 enrichment jobs for the 3,532 placeholder-cleanup people (Lime + YoopKnows). The Trigger.dev enrichment-processor task processed 1 job (31 people, status=complete) then PAUSED the remaining 19 visible jobs (each 100 people, all status=paused). Zero jobs currently running.

All 20 jobs created today at 09:32-09:33 UTC have workspaceSlug=null — they're not tagged to Lime or YoopKnows specifically.

This blocks:
- Lime E2 and E3 email campaigns (350 people need real emails before re-adding to lists)
- YoopKnows E2 campaign (1,678 people need real emails)
- Any future enrichment work

Investigate: (1) Why does the processor pause after 1 job? Rate limit? Error? Concurrency issue? (2) Check Trigger.dev logs for the enrichment-processor task. (3) The jobs have workspaceSlug=null — is the processor filtering by workspace and skipping null? (4) Is there a batch/concurrency limit that causes auto-pause? (5) Yesterday's BL-004 had the same pattern — paused at 140/400. This is the same root cause.

Fix required: The enrichment processor must process ALL queued jobs sequentially without pausing. If a job fails, log the error and move to the next job — don't pause the entire queue.

---

The BL-027 rawResponse fix addressed a different root cause (stale working-tree fix had been sitting uncommitted since session 50, only Serper Maps call site made it into git). items.title: Bulk enrichment APIs — 5-10x speed improvement
items.notes:
Current enrichment is one-person-at-a-time. Providers support bulk:

- Prospeo: /bulk-enrich-person — 50 people per request, same credit cost, returns matched/not_matched/invalid. Accepts name+company, LinkedIn URL, or email. Response includes full person+company data.
- BounceBan: /verify/bulk — 5 req/sec bulk verification
- Kitt: non-realtime batch mode (batches of 15)
- FindyMail: check if bulk endpoint exists

Impact: 1,000 people currently takes 5-10 hours. With Prospeo bulk (50/batch), the email-finding step drops to ~20 batches = minutes. Verification bulk would also be dramatically faster.

Implementation:
1. Add bulkEnrichPerson() to Prospeo adapter
2. Modify enrichment queue to chunk 50 people and call bulk API
3. Parse batch response, map back to individual person records
4. Add bulk verification for BounceBan
5. Update waterfall to use bulk where available, single as fallback

This would make enrichment near-instant for batch runs instead of hours.

items.title: Batch ICP scoring — send multiple people per Claude call
items.notes:
PARTIALLY DONE — batch function exists but not wired into CLI.

scorePersonIcpBatch() was built in src/lib/icp/scorer.ts (commit f319ecce) with batchSize=15, Claude Code CLI path. BUT scripts/cli/list-score.ts still calls the OLD single-person scoring path. The CLI script was not updated to use the new batch function.

Remaining work: Update list-score.ts to import and call scorePersonIcpBatch() instead of the per-person loop. The function signature is in scorer.ts line 335+. It accepts workspace slug, person IDs array, and options { batchSize, forceRecrawl }.

This is why the benchmark on 117 people is still running slow — it's using the old code path.

---
OBSOLETE 2026-04-11: Superseded by architectural shift. The plan was to batch multiple leads per Claude Code CLI call to reduce spawn overhead. 2026-04-11 investigation showed that scoring via Claude Code CLI was the wrong path entirely — temperature=1 default caused 10-12 point score variance even on identical input. The fix was to move scoring to the direct Anthropic API path via the existing scorePersonIcp() function with temperature: 0, which costs ~$0.001/lead (~$5 per 5000 leads) and produces byte-identical output across runs. This happens via the new scripts/cli/score-all-unscored.ts backfill tool and the scorer now opts in to persistLowConfidence: false to filter low-quality scores before DB persist. BL-012's original CLI-batching approach is not useful in the new architecture.

items.title: Cache Firecrawl website scrapes per domain during scoring
items.notes:
During ICP scoring, the scorer crawls each person's company website via Firecrawl to get context. If 5 people work at the same company, it crawls the same domain 5 times. Firecrawl credits are limited and each scrape takes 2-5 seconds.

Fix: cache crawl results per companyDomain. Before scraping, check if we've already crawled that domain in this scoring session (or within last 24h). If yes, reuse the cached markdown.

There may already be a crawl cache (getCrawlMarkdown in src/lib/icp/crawl-cache.ts) — check if it's domain-keyed or person-keyed. If person-keyed, change to domain-keyed.

Impact: For 1210's 2,016 people across ~800 unique domains, this would eliminate ~60% of Firecrawl calls. Combined with BL-012 (batch scoring), the full scoring run drops from 5.5 hours to under 30 minutes.

items.title: Replace Firecrawl with free self-hosted website scraping for ICP scoring
items.notes:
Firecrawl credits exhausted and the service costs £14-297/mo depending on plan. Most B2B company homepages are static marketing sites that don't need JS rendering.

Fix: Replace the Firecrawl call in crawl-cache.ts with a simple fetch() + HTML-to-markdown conversion (cheerio/turndown or manual tag stripping). Keep Firecrawl as optional fallback if FIRECRAWL_API_KEY is set.

Impact: Eliminates Firecrawl cost entirely for 80-90% of company sites. ICP scoring depends on website context for accurate scores — without it, Claude only has job title + company name + industry which is insufficient.

Scope: Small — modify getCrawlMarkdownInner() in src/lib/icp/crawl-cache.ts to try free fetch first, fall back to Firecrawl if available.

items.title: Route Monty QA + Security subagents to Sonnet (default Opus → Sonnet)
items.notes:
PROBLEM: Monty QA + Security subagents default to Opus. Over 48h of 04-08/09 they consumed ~20M rate-limit weight mostly on Opus. QA produces structured JSON findings (severity/category/description/suggestion), Security produces OWASP-style reports — neither needs Opus reasoning.

FIX:
1. scripts/monty-qa.ts — set model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6' as default
2. scripts/monty-security.ts — same
3. scripts/monty-orchestrator.ts — when delegating to QA/Security, pass model override
4. Document in .claude/rules/monty-orchestrator-rules.md: "QA + Security default to Sonnet. Dev keeps Opus. Override only with justification."

EXPECTED SAVINGS: ~8-12M rlt per heavy dev day, 3-4x per-QA-session.

STATUS POST-2026-04-11: P2 (was P1). Deferred because other cleanups (MEMORY.md trim, scoring off Max plan) reduced the urgency. Still worth doing when there's a chance to revisit the Monty agent definitions.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: Resolved by architecture change. Monty agents now run as Claude Code sub-agents (Max plan) instead of via runAgent() (API). When spawning QA/Security agents, model can be set to sonnet via the Agent tool's model parameter. No code change needed — the runAgent() API path is deprecated.

items.title: Title artefact cleanup — plural job titles on emailbison-synced leads (Lime, possibly others)
items.notes:
PROBLEM: Discovered during 2026-04-11 Lime score audit — several Lime leads have jobTitle values like "Warehouse Managers" (plural), "Shift Managers" (plural), "Factory Managers" (plural), "Logistics Managers" (plural). These are data artefacts from the EmailBison sync path where the source system stored pluralised job family names instead of individual titles.

NOT scoring-fatal — Haiku recognises them as manager-tier titles and scores correctly. But they'll look unprofessional in live email sends ("Hi Warehouse Managers,...") if merge-tags use jobTitle directly in copy.

SCOPE: likely only affects Lime because it's the main workspace with heavy EmailBison sync usage. Should query across all workspaces to confirm.

FIX:
1. One-off SQL update: WHERE jobTitle IN ('Warehouse Managers','Shift Managers','Factory Managers','Logistics Managers','Production Managers','Operations Managers') SET jobTitle = singular equivalent
2. Broader: normalise job titles at ingestion — detect common plurals and strip the 's'
3. Ideally: fix the EmailBison sync endpoint to not blindly copy source field values without sanitisation (BL-029 adjacent)

BLOCKED BY: nothing. Standalone.
PRIORITY: P3 — cosmetic, not blocking. Flag for next week's data quality pass.

items.title: LinkedIn company page fallback for ICP crawl cache (reduce 17% silent crawl failure rate)
items.notes:
PROBLEM: Discovered during 2026-04-11 YoopKnows scoring run — 17% of crawl attempts fail (free fetch returns <200 chars or errors, Firecrawl fallback also fails because credits are exhausted). When crawl fails, Haiku scores on title + company name alone, producing lower-quality scores (usually low-confidence, so the persistLowConfidence gate skips them, and the lead stays unscored).

Measured breakdown from YoopKnows run:
- Free fetch OK: 1,162 (83%)
- Free fetch failed + Firecrawl attempted: 242 (17%)
- Firecrawl also failed: 242 (100% of fallback attempts)

FIX: Add LinkedIn company page fallback to src/lib/icp/crawl-cache.ts getCrawlMarkdownInner():
1. Try free fetch on companyDomain
2. If fails or <200 chars: derive linkedin.com/company/{slug} from companyDomain (or from lead's linkedinUrl), fetch and extract /about text
3. If LinkedIn also fails: return null, caller must handle (current behaviour — lead skipped as low-confidence)
4. Retry policy: weekly cron picks up crawl_failed records and tries again

EXPECTED IMPACT: Drops crawl failure rate from 17% → 3-5%, which means +12% of leads get scored instead of skipped. On YoopKnows's 4,889 leads that's ~600 more contactable leads.

NOT blocking Monday. Log as next-week data quality item.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: LinkedIn company page fallback added to crawl-cache.ts. Cascade reordered to: free fetch > LinkedIn > Firecrawl (saves credits).

items.title: Outsignal per-campaign ICP scoring architecture — parked for design
items.notes:
PROBLEM: Discovered during 2026-04-11 Outsignal audit. Outsignal is a lead-gen agency running campaigns for multiple clients/verticals simultaneously. A single workspace-level icpCriteriaPrompt cannot capture this because different campaigns target completely different personas:
- Campaign [US] Staffing B2B → targets US recruitment firm owners/partners
- Campaign [US] M&A → targets US investment firm partners/VPs
- Campaign [UK] Commercial Cleaners → targets UK cleaning business directors
- Campaign [UK] Roofing Contractors → targets UK roofing contractors
- etc.

CURRENT STATE: Outsignal has 5,980 leads in the Person table, 5,945 of them unscored, because any ICP prompt that tried to cover all these verticals would reject most and accept some randomly.

DECISION 2026-04-11: Parked. Outsignal is added to the EXCLUDED_WORKSPACES set in src/lib/audit/invariants.ts so it doesn't count against system invariant totals. This is a structural architecture decision, not a data cleanup task.

DESIGN OPTIONS (for next week):
A) Campaign.icpCriteriaPrompt — per-campaign override, scoring happens per-campaign-list
B) TargetList.icpCriteriaPrompt — per-list override, allows sharing across campaigns
C) Campaign.clientBrief — free-form brief, Haiku derives rubric on the fly (expensive, non-deterministic)
D) Hybrid: per-campaign prompt for outsignal, per-workspace prompt for regular clients, explicit flag on workspace

RECOMMENDED: Option A. Simplest model, explicit per-campaign prompt, cleanest data model.

NEXT STEP: Write a design doc exploring A/B/C/D with pros/cons, schema changes, effort estimates. Do NOT implement this weekend.

ASSESSMENT (2026-04-13): Still parked. Outsignal workspace has no active campaigns. Design doc exists. Will implement when campaigns resume. Not blocking any client work.

items.title: ICP scoring temperature=0 + persistLowConfidence gate + Rise prompt
items.notes:
DONE 2026-04-11 — commit 302d4f6b.

Three changes to src/lib/icp/scorer.ts scorePersonIcp():
1. Pin temperature: 0 for deterministic output. Verified via raw SDK test: 10/10 calls produce byte-identical output at temp=0. The earlier 10-12 point variance observed during 2026-04-11 audit was caused by default temperature (~1.0), not inherent LLM non-determinism.
2. Add optional { persistLowConfidence?: boolean } option (default true preserves existing behaviour). When false, confidence="low" scores are NOT persisted — lead stays icpScore=null for retry when upstream data improves.
3. Return type: IcpScoreResult & { persisted: boolean }.

Rise prompt: written and committed to Workspace.icpCriteriaPrompt based on actual EmailBison campaigns (not aspirational workspace metadata). Targets B2B SaaS/tech companies with two personas: marketing managers and office/people ops managers. Based on direct review of the 5 active Rise campaigns and 75-lead sample.

Scripts that use the new gate: scripts/cli/score-all-unscored.ts (opts in to persistLowConfidence: false).

Usage: scored YoopKnows/MyAcq/BlankTag/Rise (in chain) during 2026-04-11 audit session. YoopKnows achieved ~85% persist rate on clean data.

items.title: Historical staging archive backfill — 12,603 Person rows that bypassed DiscoveredPerson
items.notes:
DONE 2026-04-11 — commit 7b25be44 (script), executed during audit session.

Context: 12,603 Person rows existed without a linked DiscoveredPerson archive row across 5 active workspaces. Bypass paths were EmailBison sync (9,242 rows, 73%), Clay imports (2,357, 19%, now retired), discovery-prospeo (522), discovery-aiark (365), manual (44), legacy discovery (73). Without archive entries, future discovery runs couldn't dedup against historical leads and would re-buy them.

Fix: scripts/cli/backfill-staging-archive.ts writes synthetic DiscoveredPerson rows with:
- personId linked to the existing Person
- promotedAt = Person.createdAt (historical date — keeps backfilled rows OUT of current billing window quota counts)
- status = 'promoted' (dedup filters recognise them)
- discoverySource copied from Person.source
- rawResponse JSON with _backfilledAt, _backfillReason, _personId

Results: 12,603 rows written in ~14 seconds total (1210: 482, MyAcq: 718, YoopKnows: 2,966, Lime: 3,848, Rise: 4,589). Zero failures. INV3 now ✓ across all active workspaces.

Excluded from backfill: outsignal (per 2026-04-11 parking decision, BL-020).

items.title: Fix reply step/campaign attribution — 90% of replies lack sequenceStep, ~6% lack campaignId
items.notes:
SYMPTOM (surfaced by Nova cross-client insights, week of 2026-04-01):
- global-insights.md L52: 'Step-attributed reply data only accounts for 41 replies out of 439 total (9.3%), meaning over 90% of replies lack step attribution.'
- lime-recruitment/campaigns.md L41: 'Either Step 1 drives the vast majority of replies but isn't being tracked, or step attribution is broken.'
- outsignal/campaigns.md L38: Step 1 dominance 71.4% vs cross-workspace 51.7% (anomalous — likely the 14 step-attributed replies are all webhook LEAD_REPLIED, skewing results).
- myacq/campaigns.md L31: '11 replies (12.0%) have no campaign attribution (unknown).'
- 1210-solutions/campaigns.md L25: 'Both replies came from unknown campaigns with no step attribution data.'

LIVE DB NUMBERS (2026-04-11 investigation, all-workspace Reply table):
- Total replies: 451
- Null sequenceStep: 406 (90.0%) — matches Nova citation exactly
- Null campaignId: 25 (5.5%)
- Breakdown by source:
  * webhook LEAD_REPLIED: 45 rows, 0 null step, 0 null campaign (WORKING)
  * webhook UNTRACKED_REPLY_RECEIVED: 10 rows, 10 null step, 10 null campaign (UPSTREAM — EB sends no scheduled_email for these)
  * poll: 15 rows, 15 null step, 14 null campaign (OUR BUG)
  * backfill: 380 rows, 380 null step, 14 null campaign (OUR BUG)
  * portal_send: 1 row (outbound, N/A)

ROOT CAUSE (CATEGORY C — PARTIAL / MIXED):

(A) Upstream, not our bug: 10 UNTRACKED_REPLY_RECEIVED webhook events (2.2% of replies). By definition these are replies EmailBison could not match to an outbound. Payload confirmed to contain no `data.scheduled_email` and no `data.campaign`. Webhook handler at src/app/api/webhooks/emailbison/route.ts:346 reads `data.scheduled_email.sequence_step_order`, which is undefined for this event type. NOT FIXABLE by us.

(B) Our code, fixable: poll-replies and backfill-all-replies paths HARDCODE sequenceStep to null. Specifically:
  * trigger/poll-replies.ts:213 — `sequenceStep: null, // polled replies don't have sequence_step_order`
  * scripts/backfill-all-replies.ts:214 — `sequenceStep: null,`

The comment 'polled replies don't have sequence_step_order' is half true: the GET /api/replies list endpoint returns flat Reply objects with only `scheduled_email_id` (no nested scheduled_email). BUT the step order IS recoverable via two additional EB API calls:
  1. GET /api/scheduled-emails/{id} → returns `sequence_step_id` (confirmed live against yoopknows/739113 during investigation).
  2. GET /api/campaigns/{campaignId}/sequence-steps → returns each step with `id` and `order`/`position` (already wired in EmailBisonClient.getSequenceSteps, maps to SequenceStep.position).

Cross-reference the two to map sequence_step_id → position. Since sequence steps per campaign are stable, the results can be cached per campaign for the duration of a poll/backfill run.

UPSERT CAVEAT: prisma.reply.upsert in both paths writes sequenceStep only in the `create` block (not `update`). This means existing null-step rows will NOT be auto-healed by a simple code fix — a one-off backfill pass will be needed against the 406 existing rows.

SCOPED FIX PLAN (est. 60-100 LOC, 3-4 files, no schema change):

1. src/lib/emailbison/client.ts — add `getScheduledEmail(id: number): Promise<ScheduledEmail>` method (~12 lines). Endpoint verified live: GET /api/scheduled-emails/{id} returns { data: { id, campaign_id, sequence_step_id, ... } }.

2. src/lib/emailbison/types.ts — add `ScheduledEmail` interface with at minimum { id, campaign_id, sequence_step_id }.

3. src/lib/emailbison/step-resolver.ts (NEW, ~30 lines) — export `resolveSequenceStepOrder(client, campaignId, scheduledEmailId)` with an in-memory per-campaign step cache. Returns step position (1-indexed) or null on any failure. Non-throwing — reply persistence must not be blocked by EB API hiccups.

4. trigger/poll-replies.ts line 213 — replace hardcoded null with `await resolveSequenceStepOrder(client, reply.campaign_id, reply.scheduled_email_id)`. Gate on reply.scheduled_email_id not null. One EB call per newly-seen reply, cached list of steps per campaign.

5. scripts/backfill-all-replies.ts line 214 — same change.

6. One-off backfill script (NEW, ~40 lines at scripts/cli/backfill-reply-step-attribution.ts) — iterate over Reply rows WHERE sequenceStep IS NULL AND source IN ('poll','backfill','webhook') AND emailBisonReplyId IS NOT NULL. For each, fetch EB reply via existing getReply, then fetch scheduled-email, resolve step position, UPDATE Reply.sequenceStep. Batch in chunks of 100 with progress logging. Excludes rows with source='webhook' AND eventType='UNTRACKED_REPLY_RECEIVED' (nothing to recover).

7. Campaign attribution gap (5.5%): the 14 null-campaignId rows in poll and 14 in backfill come from reply.campaign_id being null on the EB side (no match) OR the Outsignal Campaign lookup by emailBisonCampaignId finding nothing. The webhook path has the same lookup logic. Investigate whether these 28 rows are genuinely unmatched on EB's side (upstream) or whether we should fall back to resolving via scheduled_email.campaign_id when reply.campaign_id is null. Likely a small second-pass improvement once the step fix lands.

OPERATIONAL IMPLICATIONS:
- Per-reply API call adds latency to poll-replies cron (runs every 10 min, typically 0-5 new replies per run). Negligible.
- Backfill script is offline / manual — no concern.
- Rate limit headroom on EB API: current poll already calls getRecentReplies + getSenderEmails for 9 workspaces concurrently. Adding one more call per new reply is safe.
- Error handling: step resolution must be non-throwing; null fallback preserves current behavior.

AFFECTED WORKSPACES: All 7 active (rise, outsignal, myacq, yoopknows, lime-recruitment, 1210-solutions, blanktag). Largest absolute impact on rise, outsignal, myacq (biggest reply volumes from the March 24 backfill run).

ACTION TAKEN (2026-04-11): FILED ONLY. Fix plan scoped and ready to implement. NOT implemented on branch fix/bl-027-rawresponse-null because fix spans 3+ files, introduces a new EB API method, requires a one-off backfill script against production data, and is outside the current branch's scope (raw-response preservation). Schedule as dedicated fix branch.

NEXT STEP FOR PM: approve standalone fix branch for BL-028 implementation. Est. 1-2 hrs dev + QA. Will unblock Nova cross-client step-level analytics and remove the 90% data gap that's been flagged in every weekly intel run since 2026-04-01.

COMPLETED: Fixed in commit 917bfb2e on 2026-04-11. Reply step and campaign attribution restored on poll/backfill paths. 262 historical rows healed by backfill-all-replies.ts.

items.title: Campaign attribution fallback: use ScheduledEmail.campaign_id when Reply.campaign_id is null

items.title: Pre-commit hook: replace hardcoded trufflehog path with command -v lookup
items.notes:
PROBLEM: .git/hooks/pre-commit hardcodes /Users/jjay/.local/bin/trufflehog as the path to the secret scanner. Any teammate (or any new machine setup) that installs the hook via docs/dev-setup.md will hit 'command not found' on their first commit because trufflehog lives elsewhere on their machine.

DISCOVERED: 2026-04-11 when the pre-commit typecheck hook (F10 / batch 1 item A) was added on top of the existing trufflehog gate. The hardcoded path pre-dated this work and wasn't introduced by it, but the new hook installation docs surfaced the issue.

FIX: In .git/hooks/pre-commit, swap the hardcoded path for a command lookup:

  TRUFFLEHOG_BIN="$(command -v trufflehog 2>/dev/null || true)"
  if [ -z "$TRUFFLEHOG_BIN" ]; then
    echo "[pre-commit] trufflehog not found on PATH — skipping secret scan"
  else
    "$TRUFFLEHOG_BIN" git file://. ...
  fi

This gracefully skips the scan if trufflehog isn't installed (prints a warning rather than blocking the commit). Anyone with it installed gets the scan automatically regardless of where it lives on their machine.

EFFORT: 2 minutes. Single-file edit.

PRIORITY: P3. Not Monday-blocking. Not currently biting anyone because only one machine uses this repo. File it now so it doesn't evaporate.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: Pre-commit hook updated to use command -v trufflehog instead of hardcoded path. Skips silently if trufflehog not installed. docs/dev-setup.md also updated.

items.title: LinkedIn sequence A/B variant support — variantKey on CampaignSequenceRule + LinkedInAction
items.notes:

Real feature must ship before then.

HARD UNKNOWN — spike first: link Reply → LinkedInAction for per-variant attribution. Today Reply attributes to sequenceStep (int), not specific action. Biggest risk in estimate; spike before committing timeline.

items.title: Harden AI Ark export webhook handler — schema validation + failure logging
items.notes:
Derived from session 50 deploy verification investigation (2026-04-10).

The new webhook at src/app/api/webhooks/aiark/export/route.ts is defensively written — extractPeople() tries 5 known payload shapes and mapExportPerson() probes nested structures safely. It won't crash on unknown payloads. However, three long-term maintainability risks remain:

1. SILENT SHAPE DRIFT: if AI Ark changes their export payload structure, the handler will silently produce zero results with no visible error. All 5 extraction paths would just return empty arrays. Add a loud warning log when all extraction paths fail AND payload is non-empty.

2. MISSING runId HANDLING: when runId is absent, the handler logs a warning and returns 200 OK without persisting anything. This prevents webhook retry loops (correct) but makes debugging impossible. Consider persisting the raw payload to a debug table (or S3/local file) for manual inspection.

3. NO SCHEMA VALIDATION: once AI Ark provides an official export payload spec (or we log the first real webhook call), add Zod schema validation against the expected shape. Current implementation is bottom-up probing — switch to top-down validation with a typed payload.

Low priority, non-blocking. Revisit after the first real AI Ark export run confirms the actual payload shape. (Recovered from stash@{0} 2026-04-11.)

items.title: Apify Leads Finder bypassing BounceBan verification
items.notes:
Extracted from stash BL-017 source-first enrichment investigation (session 50, 2026-04-09/10).

(Extracted from stash BL-017 investigation during 2026-04-11 stash recovery.)

items.title: Pipeline reorder: score ICP before enrichment, gate enrichment on score threshold
items.notes:
PROBLEM: The current discovery → enrichment → scoring flow pays enrichment credits on every promoted lead, regardless of whether that lead would have passed ICP scoring. This means ~30-40% of leads (the ones that score below the threshold after enrichment) represent wasted enrichment spend — we paid Prospeo/FindyMail/Kitt/BounceBan to find and verify emails for leads we were always going to reject.

CURRENT FLOW:
  Discovery adapter → DiscoveredPerson staging → promoteDiscoveredLeads() → Person row created → enrichment waterfall (Prospeo bulk + FindyMail parallel + Kitt fallback + BounceBan verify) → scorePersonIcp() → score stored on PersonWorkspace → campaign-eligible

TARGET FLOW:
  Discovery adapter → DiscoveredPerson staging → scorePersonIcp() → gate on workspace.icpScoreThreshold → if score ≥ threshold → promoteDiscoveredLeads() → enrichment waterfall (only for passing leads) → contactable Person row → campaign-eligible

WHY NOT DONE DURING 2026-04-11 WEEKEND AUDIT SESSION: Considered but deferred. The architectural reorder touches promotion.ts, waterfall.ts, enrichment-processor.ts (Trigger.dev), and the scoring call sites. It's a 1-2 day focused refactor, not a weekend patch. The weekend session focused on data cleanup + invariant enforcement instead, which was the right call given the time budget and the other fragility issues that needed fixing first.

EXPECTED IMPACT:
- Enrichment credit savings: ~$30-60/month based on 2026-04-11 per-workspace volumes
  - Current: enriching ~3,000 leads per backfill run × 6 workspaces × ~$0.005/lead = ~$90/run
  - Target: enriching ~1,800 high-fit leads per backfill × same = ~$54/run, saving ~$36/run
  - At ~2 backfill runs per month per workspace = ~$72/month saved across active workspaces
- Cleaner data model: Person table becomes "contactable, high-fit lead" rather than "any lead we tried to enrich"
- Simpler audit semantics: INV2 scoring coverage becomes "everything in Person is scored" by construction rather than "every PersonWorkspace has icpScore" as an invariant to enforce
- Faster discovery-to-campaign cycle time: no longer blocking on enrichment for leads that will be rejected anyway

REQUIRED CHANGES:
1. src/lib/discovery/promotion.ts — move scorePersonIcp() call from post-enrichment to pre-enrichment. Scoring needs to operate on DiscoveredPerson-stage data (name, title, companyDomain, location) rather than Person-stage enriched data.
2. Add workspace.icpScoreThreshold read at the top of the promotion flow. If the workspace doesn't have one set (most don't today), default to 40 (matching current implicit threshold behaviour).
3. Gate the enrichment trigger on score passing threshold. Leads below threshold stay in DiscoveredPerson with status='scored_rejected' (new status value, needs DB enum update if enum — check schema).
4. src/lib/enrichment/waterfall.ts — no behavior change to the waterfall itself, but its callers need to only invoke it on passing leads.
5. trigger/enrichment-processor.ts — the Trigger.dev task that processes enrichment jobs. Needs to filter out below-threshold leads from its queue.
6. Handle the existing 19,980 active-workspace leads: they're already past this gate (they're in Person with scores), so they don't need migration. New discoveries going forward get the new flow.
7. scripts/cli/score-all-unscored.ts — the backfill script. It currently scores Person rows; under the new flow it should either be renamed to score-discovered-persons.ts or kept as-is for the edge case of rescoring existing Person rows.

RISKS / OPEN QUESTIONS:
- Scoring currently has access to company.crawlMarkdown which comes from the crawl cache (BL-013/BL-014). Pre-promotion scoring means the crawl happens earlier in the pipeline. This is fine architecturally but changes when crawl API calls fire — worth measuring crawl cost impact.
- If scoring fails (transient API error, low-confidence skip), what happens to the DiscoveredPerson row? New state machine needed: staged → scoring_pending → scored_passing | scored_rejected | scoring_failed (retry).
- Workspaces with null icpCriteriaPrompt (outsignal, and any new workspace before its prompt is written) would have ALL leads fail scoring. Need fallback: if no prompt, skip scoring and promote directly to maintain backwards compat, OR block promotion entirely until a prompt exists. Product decision needed.
- ICP score threshold default of 40: captures "anyone 40+ is worth enriching" — but is 40 the right cutoff? May want to measure ICP score distribution per workspace to tune. Not Monday-blocking.

DEPENDENCIES:
- BL-021 (DiscoveredPerson.rawResponse null fix) — SHIPPED 2026-04-11 via commit 0e3dc11a + 172f6e5a. Pipeline reorder relies on staging rows having usable rawResponse data for source-first enrichment post-promotion.
- BL-031 (runner.ts architecture review) — unrelated, parallel track.
- BL-032 (dormant wrapper smoke test) — unrelated, parallel track.

TIMELINE: 1-2 days focused work. Should ship as its own phase, not squeezed into Batch 2/3 of the fragility fix work. Candidate for early next week.

PRIORITY: P2. Medium severity. Not Monday-blocking for current customer work — the current flow produces correct data, just with ~$72/month of wasted enrichment spend and a less clean data model. Worth shipping in the next 2 weeks to capture the savings and simplify the architecture before more code gets built on top of the current pattern.

PROMOTED TO P1 (2026-04-13): Source-first enrichment and score-before-enrichment are both critical for credit efficiency. Wasting enrichment credits on leads that will be rejected is unacceptable at scale.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: scoreStagedPersonIcpBatch() scores leads pre-promotion using Claude Code CLI (Haiku). Promotion gate rejects below threshold (default 40). Fail-open on errors. QA reviewed, 6 findings fixed.

items.title: Nova memory architecture — decide which files should be git-tracked
items.notes:
The current .gitignore rule `.nova/memory/**/*.md` treats all Nova memory as ephemeral, but operational records like campaigns.md (live campaign IDs, target list shards, sender warmup state) and the cohort pointers in learnings.md are load-bearing state that needs git tracking. On 2026-04-11 three files were force-added as one-off exceptions during stash recovery (.nova/memory/blanktag/campaigns.md, .nova/memory/yoopknows/learnings.md, .nova/memory/lime-recruitment/learnings.md). This item captures the architectural question: which Nova memory files should be categorically tracked vs gitignored?

Options to consider:

1. Split by filename: track campaigns.md + profile.md, gitignore feedback.md + learnings.md (mixed operational + ephemeral)
2. Split by directory: track .nova/memory/<slug>/operational/ subdirectory, gitignore the rest
3. Split by content marker: each file opens with a # TRACKED or # EPHEMERAL header, .gitattributes filter respects it
4. Split by explicit allowlist: gitignore pattern flipped to .nova/memory/**/*.md except for specific named files

Decision needed before any more Nova memory force-adds. Scope: ~30-60 min decision doc + gitignore update + brief retrofit if the chosen rule requires moving files.

ASSESSMENT (2026-04-13): Nova memory files (.nova/memory/{slug}/*.md) are currently force-added to git as one-off exceptions. Decision needed: should all Nova memory be git-tracked (version history, cross-agent visibility) or gitignored (ephemeral, agent-only)? Current state works but is inconsistent.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: Current design is correct. .md files gitignored (prevents client intelligence in version control), .gitkeep tracked (preserves directory structure). Memory loads from disk at runtime via loadMemoryContext() — git tracking not required. Selective force-adds during stash recovery are intentional exceptions. No change needed.

items.title: AI Ark search source-first enrichment — sourceId captured but never used for direct lookup
items.notes:
GAP: When AI Ark search discovers a person, promotion.ts (line ~300) extracts and stores their AI Ark person ID as PersonWorkspace.sourceId. However, the waterfall's source-first block (waterfall.ts lines 683-689) only routes Prospeo-sourced leads for direct lookup — AI Ark search leads fall through to the generic waterfall. The sourceId is captured but wasted.

CURRENT BEHAVIOR: AI Ark search -> staging -> promotion (sourceId stored) -> enrichment waterfall -> AI Ark person enrichment runs as step 1 of the generic waterfall (coincidental, not source-first). This works but doesn't use the stored ID for a cheaper/faster direct lookup.

EXPECTED BEHAVIOR: All discovery sources (Prospeo, AI Ark, Leads Finder) should try to source email/LinkedIn from their own platform first using the stored sourceId, only falling back to the generic waterfall if the source platform fails.

FIX: Add AI Ark source-first path in waterfall.ts source-first block (lines 683-689). When discoverySource === 'aiark' and sourceId exists, call AI Ark person enrichment endpoint directly with the stored ID before entering the generic waterfall. Pattern matches existing Prospeo source-first implementation.

RELATED: BL-038 (pipeline reorder). Both items target enrichment credit efficiency. Should be implemented together.

LEADS FINDER: Returns verified emails directly so doesn't need source-first for email. LinkedIn URL preservation should be verified separately.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: bulkEnrichByAiArkId() uses /v1/people/export/single with stored sourceId. Wired into batch + single waterfall paths. Runtime type validation on API response. Existing staged AI Ark records have null rawResponse (pre-BL-027) — source-first works for new discoveries only.

items.title: CRITICAL: LinkedIn connection requests not executing — BlankTag (468 pending) + Lime (1,516 pending), profile_views working but connection_requests stuck at pending
items.notes:
P1 — campaigns deployed but effectively dormant. Profile views completing (17 BlankTag, 59 Lime in 7 days) but ZERO connection requests completed. 1,984 total pending connection requests across both workspaces. Blocks all downstream sequence progression (messages, follow-ups). Investigation delegated to Dev Agent — checking worker code, sequencer logic, session health, rate limiting config.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: Root cause was BL-042 (batch starvation). Fixed by making getNextBatch() query each action type independently with per-type budget checks. Deployed to production.

items.title: LinkedIn action type starvation — connection requests never picked up while profile views fill batch
items.notes:
ROOT CAUSE: queue.ts lines 110-142 — getNextBatch() fetches ALL pending actions regardless of type, ordered by priority + scheduledFor. Then applies budget checks sequentially.

PROBLEM:
- Profile views and connection requests mixed in the same batch
- Profile views have 2-2.5x higher daily limits (10-50/day vs 5-20/day for connections)
- If profile_view actions are scheduled earlier, they fill the batch first
- By the time connection_requests come up, batch limit is reached or worker cycle ends
- Budget system is per-type (separate counters) but selection is type-agnostic
- Result: connection requests never get picked up while profile views keep flowing

IMPACT: BlankTag and Lime LinkedIn campaigns are effectively dormant — connection requests are not being sent.

FIX NEEDED: Make getNextBatch() action-type-aware. Options:
  A) Separate queries per action type, interleave results
  B) Weighted batch selection guaranteeing both types fair allocation
  C) Round-robin: alternate between action types across cycles

FILE: src/lib/linkedin/queue.ts

DIAGNOSED BY: Other Claude Code session investigating LinkedIn campaign dormancy on 2026-04-13.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: Rewrote getNextBatch() to query each action type group independently (connections, views, messages). Each type gets up to perTypeLimit actions per poll cycle, budget-checked against its own daily limit. No shared pool. Worker calls limit=2 so each cycle returns up to 6 actions (2 per type). Connection requests can never be starved by profile views.

items.title: Activity page shows createdAt instead of receivedAt — backfilled replies appear as 'Yesterday' when they're weeks old
items.notes:
SYMPTOM: Rise Activity page shows alex@gierd.com reply as 'Yesterday' but EmailBison shows it from March 15 (29 days ago). The reply was ingested via backfill on April 11.

ROOT CAUSE: Activity page uses createdAt for all action types. This is wrong — each action type has a different 'when did this actually happen' field:
- Replies: receivedAt (when the prospect replied)
- Profile views / connection requests: completedAt (when the worker executed)
- Emails sent: sentAt or equivalent from EmailBison
- LinkedIn messages: completedAt

FIX: Activity page needs a computed 'occurredAt' per action type, not a blanket createdAt. Options:
A) Compute occurredAt in the API query (CASE/COALESCE per type)
B) Add a denormalized occurredAt field populated on write
C) Split the Activity feed by action type, each using its correct timestamp

SCOPE: This affects the Activity page component + its data-fetching query. Need to check how the page currently queries and renders actions to determine the right approach.

AFFECTED: Any workspace with backfilled replies or actions that were ingested with lag.

COMPLETED 2026-04-13: email-adapter.ts changed from createdAt to receivedAt for performedAt. 2-line fix. LinkedIn adapter was already correct (completedAt).

items.title: Writer-side quality validation should run pre-publish, not block client approval
items.notes:
The "CTA must be a question" rule is from writer-rules.md, intended to catch writer errors before content reaches a client. Today it surfaced as a hard block in the CLIENT approval flow (Lime E5, 2026-04-14). Even if the validator were correct, the client should never see internal writer rule violations — they should be caught and remediated pre-publish.

Two sub-decisions: (a) move writer-validator errors to a pre-publish gate that prevents a campaign from being published if it fails, and (b) decide whether the client-portal validator should exist at all (probably soft warnings only, never hard blocks).

Related to BL-050 — fixing BL-050 alone treats the symptom; BL-051 fixes the design. Should be considered together.

items.title: Admin LinkedIn activity UI: distinguish 'Queued' from 'Executing' state
items.notes:
Reproducible at /portal/admin LinkedIn activity views — the current 'In Progress' label on a LinkedInAction with status='running' is misleading. The worker batch-fetches up to 5 actions per type per poll and marks them ALL as 'running' simultaneously, but executes them serially with a 3-30 minute spread delay. So users see 18-20 actions appear as 'In Progress' within minutes when actually only 1 per sender is firing on LinkedIn at any moment.

Witnessed 2026-04-14: Claudia panicked seeing 20 BlankTag actions 'In Progress' in 7 minutes. Investigation confirmed only 2 had actually fired on LinkedIn (correct, within daily limits) — the other 18 were queued in the worker's memory awaiting their spread-delay turn.

Fix: split status='running' into two display states in the UI:
- 'Queued' — pulled from queue, marked running in DB, waiting in worker memory
- 'Executing' — actively talking to LinkedIn right now (max 1 per sender)

May require a new field on LinkedInAction (e.g. executingStartedAt) or a separate 'currently executing per sender' tracking mechanism. Alternative: just rename the label to 'Queued' since that's what 95%+ of 'running' rows actually are at any moment.

This is UX only — the system is functioning correctly and respects LinkedIn safety limits. Low priority but causes user confusion and panic.

items.title: Sequence rewrite shouldn't carry forward content/leads approval flags
items.notes: Discovered 2026-04-14 on 1210 Healthcare. Nova Writer rewrote both Healthcare email + LinkedIn sequences per Jamie's feedback. After save, the Campaign rows still showed `contentApproved: true` from the OLD copy's approval. Client hadn't seen the new copy but it was flagged as approved. Risk: any rewrite without explicit flag reset will silently carry old approvals forward and could deploy unreviewed content to production. Fix: in the save-sequence path (Nova Writer's save), set `contentApproved: false` whenever a sequence is overwritten on a campaign that previously had contentApproved=true. Same for leadsApproved if the target list changed (lower priority — content rewrites are more common). Add an integration test that rewrites a sequence and asserts contentApproved gets reset. Also consider an audit log entry on every approval flag change so we can trace 'who approved what' historically. Manual reset performed for the 2 affected Healthcare campaigns via scripts/maintenance/reset-healthcare-content-approved.ts (BL-052 ID was already taken, so this entry is BL-053).

items.title: Planner dedup ignores migration-debris cancellations, re-invites already-invited profiles
items.notes: Root cause: 2026-04-13 pull-model cutover's `scripts/maintenance/cancel-stale-pull-migration-pendings.ts` bulk-cancelled push-era pending `LinkedInAction` rows as `pre-pull-model-migration-debris`. Those rows had already been delivered to LinkedIn (3-week invite retention). Planner's exclusion query in `src/app/api/linkedin/plan/route.ts` used `status NOT IN ('cancelled','expired')`, so debris rows became invisible and the same people got re-planned. The 21-day cooldown was a design-doc concept never enforced in SQL. Surfaced: 2026-04-15 via recurring `already_invited` errors on James (BlankTag) and one older Lucy (Lime) case. 14 queued time-bombs identified across blanktag + lime-recruitment. Fix shipped in two commits: `4525b3f4` (one-shot backfill script `scripts/maintenance/backfill-already-invited-cooldown.ts` cancelled the 14 pending rows with reason `already-invited-cooldown-backfill`) and `91270ba8` (planner SQL rewrite — both SELECT blocks in route.ts now branch on action type: `connect`/`connection_request` workspace-wide with 21-day cooldown regardless of status; `profile_view` retains campaign-scoped dedup). 9 new tests in `src/__tests__/linkedin-plan-sql.test.ts`. Deployed to Vercel 2026-04-15 ~08:50 London. Follow-ups filed: BL-055 (exclusionEntry test mock gap), BL-056 (21d cooldown as named constant), BL-057 (planner SQL integration tests).

items.title: Planner SQL integration tests against real/staging DB

All "integration tests" against `/api/linkedin/plan` raw-SQL paths only verify that the JS calls Prisma — they don't verify the SQL actually does what it claims against a real Postgres. Should be runnable locally and in CI pre-deploy. Cover at minimum: 21-day cooldown branch, cancelled-row exclusion bug class, profile_view campaign scoping, workspace scoping. Reference `scripts/maintenance/_bl054-live-sql-check.ts` (untracked) for the live-DB read-only pattern Monty used during BL-054 to validate.

Acceptance: an integration suite exists, runs against a Postgres (test DB or staging), covers the 4 BL-054 test cases at the SQL level, runs in CI before Vercel deploy.

items.title: LinkedIn rate-limiter — consumeBudget retry double-count + jitter allows over-base
items.notes:
DONE. Shipped in commit 06707ac5. Surfaced from 2026-04-15 James BlankTag audit.

Bug 1 (consumeBudget retry double-count): `/api/linkedin/actions/[id]/complete` called markComplete then consumeBudget unconditionally. Retried actions hit the endpoint again and re-incremented `LinkedInDailyUsage`. James's connectionsSent showed 5 today with only 3 actually-completed actions — 2 bogus increments from retries. Fix: only consume budget when prior status was 'running' (mirror the /fail route's `wasRunning` gate). Smallest blast radius — no schema change, no migration. 4 new tests in `src/__tests__/linkedin-complete-route.test.ts`.

Bug 2 (jitter direction allows over-base): `applyJitter` used `base * (1 + factor)` with factor in `(-0.2, 0.2)`, letting effective cap rise above configured base (base 6 → up to 7). Mathematical enabler for yesterday's 9/6 overshoot remaining "in range". Fix: flipped to `base * (1 - factor)` with factor in `[0, 0.2)` — configured limit is now a hard ceiling. 5 new tests locking the invariant. Comment on existing running-count test at line 443 updated to reflect new max = base (was `≈ 7.2 → 7`).

Worker deploy not required: worker/src/api-client.ts calls /complete over HTTP; rate-limiter.ts is not vendored into the Railway bundle. Single Vercel deploy covers both fixes.

Local verification: tsc clean, 55 BL-058 tests pass (51 rate-limiter + 4 complete-route), full suite 709 pass / 37 pre-existing BL-055 exclusionEntry failures (unchanged baseline).

items.title: Zombie deploy compensating transaction in initiateCampaignDeploy
items.notes: Surfaced from BL-061 QA Finding A. If `prisma.campaignDeploy.create()` succeeds and `tasks.trigger('campaign-deploy', ...)` subsequently throws (Trigger.dev outage, auth rotation, network), the Campaign is left in `status='deployed'` with a CampaignDeploy row `status='pending'` that no worker will pick up. BL-061 mitigation was at the CLI layer only (try/catch + synthetic failure row + loud stderr `zombie deploy` warning + halt). The helper itself still has the same window as pre-refactor. Fix sketch: wrap the updateMany + CampaignDeploy.create + tasks.trigger in a logical transaction where a trigger throw causes: (a) campaign.status revert to 'approved' via updateMany guard, (b) CampaignDeploy.delete or mark as 'failed_to_dispatch', (c) rethrow so CLI still stops on failure. OR: keep the trigger outside the transaction but add a scheduled reconciler that detects CampaignDeploys stuck in pending with no worker heartbeat. Acceptance: a trigger-throws test in deploy-campaign.test.ts passes with campaign back to 'approved' and no orphan CampaignDeploy row.

items.title: CLI integration test coverage for main()/stdin in campaign-deploy.ts
items.notes: Surfaced from BL-061 QA Finding B. Current campaign-deploy-cli.test.ts covers: splitStdinIds, parseCliArgs, classifyResult, and the Finding-A helper-throw path. Not covered: the stdin → splitStdinIds → parseCliArgs wiring end-to-end, the serialized for-loop with real-ish verdicts, the post-verify success path populating verifiedStatus/verifiedDeployedAt, the runWithHarness envelope output shape. Fix sketch: add `main()` integration tests that mock initiateCampaignDeploy + getCampaign and drive stdin via `process.stdin` push, assert the envelope shape on stdout, assert stop-on-first-failure when a verdict is `ok:false`. Acceptance: campaign-deploy-cli.test.ts has at least 2 additional tests covering stdin end-to-end + normal stop-on-first-failure (complementing the helper-throw test already there).

items.title: Missing race test: updateMany count=0 with pre-load status='approved' in initiateCampaignDeploy
items.notes: Surfaced from BL-061 QA Finding C. Helper disambiguates `already_deployed` vs `not_approved` when updateMany returns count=0 by inspecting the ALREADY-LOADED campaign's status. If the load happened at status='approved' but a concurrent deploy flipped it before updateMany landed, the loaded status is still 'approved' — so the helper returns `not_approved` even though the true state is `already_deployed`. Misleading error code for that exact race. Fix sketch: on updateMany count=0, refetch the campaign post-updateMany to disambiguate, OR acknowledge the ambiguity in a code comment and accept the misleading code as low-impact. Add a test variant where the loaded campaign.status is 'approved' but updateMany returns count=0, asserting the chosen behaviour. Acceptance: the race is either test-covered with correct disambiguation, or explicitly documented as acceptable drift.

items.title: Drop unused `export` on SummaryEnvelope in campaign-deploy.ts

items.title: Automate EmailBison UI completion — schedule / senders / tracking via undocumented EB API
items.notes:
PROJECT BRIEF. Surfaced during BL-061 17-campaign batch deploy (2026-04-15). Fundamental platform gap: after `POST /api/campaigns/{id}/deploy` lands leads into EmailBison, a human still has to open the EB UI per campaign to: (1) set sending schedule (days, times, timezone), (2) configure sender overrides or allowlists, (3) toggle link_tracking / stop_on_reply / open_tracking. Every campaign — 17 today, dozens per week in steady state — needs this manual touch. Not a one-off; it's the platform ceiling on throughput.

Scope (research phase first):
1. RESEARCH — inspect EmailBison UI network calls via browser devtools to find undocumented endpoints. Document: endpoint URLs, HTTP methods, request/response shapes, auth model (same bearer as existing EmailBison client? session cookie? separate token?). The existing EmailBison client at src/lib/emailbison/client.ts uses bearer auth for the documented API subset — extending it is the natural fit if endpoints are bearer-auth compatible.
2. PROTOTYPE — extend `src/lib/emailbison/client.ts` with schedule/senders/tracking setters. Start with the safest endpoint (schedule — time-based config, reversible, least blast radius).
3. INTEGRATE — wire the new setters into `src/lib/campaigns/deploy.ts` (executeDeploy orchestrator) so a deploy finishes with schedule + sender + tracking applied, not just leads uploaded.
4. CLI surface — consider extending `scripts/cli/campaign-deploy.ts` with a `--post-configure` flag (or a sibling `campaign-configure.ts`) that applies the settings from Campaign row config to an already-deployed EB campaign.

Not today. File as a follow-up project because (a) research needs devtools inspection on a live EB session, (b) undocumented APIs are fragile — belongs behind tests + monitoring, (c) deploying 17 campaigns with an untested UI-automation layer is two risks bundled. Today: land BL-061 CLI, run the 17 deploys, ops manually completes UI config per the existing runbook. Tomorrow: start BL-066 research.

Acceptance (project scope, not a single commit):
- Documented EB UI endpoints in `docs/emailbison-ui-api.md` with request/response schemas and auth model
- EmailBison client extended with typed methods for schedule/senders/tracking
- Integration with deploy pipeline OR sibling CLI tool
- Tests — mocked unit tests for the new client methods; integration test against a disposable EB sandbox campaign
- Monitoring — alerts if an undocumented endpoint returns 4xx (sign that EB shipped a UI change that broke us)
- Runbook update — `docs/runbooks/campaign-deploy-post-steps.md` either updated to reflect automation, or deprecated if fully automated.

items.title: LinkedIn deploy fails — prisma.campaignSequenceRule.createMany schema mismatch
items.notes:
Captured from 4 failed 1210 LinkedIn deploys on 2026-04-15 12:18 UTC (CampaignDeploy.error + linkedinError on rows cmo00noqq001dp8j58oixz8jj, cmo00nm600017p8j5snmaqr6u, cmo00nl6v0014p8j5noxagu1g, cmo00njq90011p8j5kyha6eda). VERBATIM TRACE:

Invalid `prisma.campaignSequenceRule.createMany()` invocation:

{
  data: [
    {
      workspaceSlug: "1210-solutions",
      campaignName: "1210 Solutions - LinkedIn - ...",
      triggerEvent: "connection_accepted",
      triggerStepRef: null,
      actionType: "message",
      messageTemplate: "...",
      delayMinutes: 4320,
      requireConnected: true,
      conditionType: null,
      conditionStepRef: null,
      elseActionType: null,
      elseMessageTemplate: null,
      elseDelayMinutes: null,
      position: undefined,
      variantKey: null,
      variantWeight: 1
    },
    ...
  ]
}

Argument `position` is missing.

ROOT CAUSE HYPOTHESIS: `CampaignSequenceRule.position Int` (prisma/schema.prisma:1110) is non-nullable with no default. The LinkedIn rule builder (likely in src/lib/channels/linkedin-adapter.ts or related sequence-rule synthesiser) passes `position: undefined` for every rule in the createMany data array. Prisma input validation rejects the call atomically — no rows written, no EB campaign created (CampaignDeploy.emailBisonCampaignId is null on all 4). Fix surface: the rule-builder must compute 1-based (or 0-based — verify pattern in existing rules) position per rule before createMany. A sibling `variantKey: null` on every rule suggests the builder also isn't grouping variants — may need review alongside the position fix.

Fix landing in Phase 2 of 2026-04-15 deploy rebuild. 4 1210 LinkedIn Campaigns rolled back in Phase 0 (Campaign status deployed → approved, CampaignDeploy status failed → rolled_back, contentApproved + leadsApproved retained); safe to re-deploy once fixed. Affected campaign IDs: cmneqa5r50003p8rk322w3vc6 (Industrial/Warehouse), cmneq1z3i0001p8ef36c814py (Green List Priority), cmneqixvz0003p871m8sw9u7o (Facilities/Cleaning), cmneq93i80001p8p78pcw4yg9 (Construction). Audit rows under action='campaign.status.bl068_rollback' entityType='Campaign'.

items.title: Channel sequence JSON shape-drift — normalise email/LinkedIn on the same key scheme
items.notes: Surfaced during BL-068 fix. Email steps stored with `position` key, LinkedIn stored with `stepNumber`. BL-068 hot-fixed LinkedIn adapter to accept both via Zod + fallback, but the underlying shape-drift should be normalised. Preferred: migrate writer/save-sequence paths so both channels use the same key (whichever matches schema: position). Requires data migration for existing LinkedIn sequences. Adjacent cleanup: delayDays vs delayHours inconsistency at linkedin-adapter.ts:90 (Phase 2 recon F3) — bundle into same fix. Also: the 24/28 null variantKey rule rows (Phase 2 recon F4) are expected-null pending A/B epic, not a bug. Phase 2 QA also surfaced the SAME silent-cast bug on two other code paths: src/app/api/linkedin/plan/route.ts:199 and src/lib/pipeline/signal-campaigns.ts:390. These are latent time-bombs — they will fire if a stepNumber-shaped LinkedIn campaign hits the signal-campaign matcher or the daily LinkedIn planner. Not a blocker for the 2026-04-15 deploy rebuild (redeploy routes through the fixed linkedin-adapter.ts), but BL-069 must cover these two call sites when picked up, not just the email/LinkedIn sequence-save-path normalisation. Phase 3 QA also found a pre-existing `as Array<{...}>` cast inside `getSequenceSteps` fallback at src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:733 (inside the JSON.parse + try/catch that now logs on failure). Out of Phase 3 scope (read path, not deploy write path) but bundle into this normalisation sweep.

items.title: Race condition — Campaign.emailBisonCampaignId missing @unique lets concurrent deploys both createCampaign
items.notes: Surfaced during Phase 3 adversarial QA on 2026-04-15 deploy rebuild (commit [pending Phase 3 main]). `prisma/schema.prisma:757` declares `emailBisonCampaignId Int?` on Campaign with `@@index` but NO `@unique` constraint. The Phase 3 Step 1 idempotency guard (src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:181-217) relies on a read-then-write pattern: SELECT Campaign -> if emailBisonCampaignId null then createCampaign on EB then UPDATE Campaign. Two concurrent executeDeploy invocations for the same campaignId (plausible triggers: Trigger.dev retry/double-dispatch, admin double-click in UI, manual CLI re-run during a long task) BOTH see emailBisonCampaignId=null in their Prisma read, BOTH call ebClient.createCampaign (EB has no caller-dedup), and BOTH persist different EB IDs — the second UPDATE wins, first EB draft is orphaned. This EXACTLY reproduces the BL-061 duplicate-EB-draft bug Phase 3 is meant to eliminate. Phase 3 could not fix due to hard no-migration rule. Fix: (a) add @unique to Campaign.emailBisonCampaignId in schema + migration, then catch P2002 at Step 2 and re-read Campaign + reuse the winner's EB ID (and DELETE the loser's EB draft via ebClient.deleteCampaign to prevent orphan); OR (b) wrap Steps 1-2 in prisma.$transaction with an advisory lock keyed on campaignId. Option (a) is cleaner. BLOCKS Phase 4 GO — concurrency test suite must cover this before re-enabling auto-deploy paths. Interim mitigation: serialize deploy invocations at the orchestrator level (deploy-campaign.ts) via an in-memory Map<campaignId, Promise> debouncer. Not foolproof across Vercel instances but reduces the attack surface. PM-owned decision.

items.title: Workspace.ebTagIds schema field missing — blocks Step 8 tag attachment in deploy flow
items.notes: Surfaced during Phase 3 deploy rebuild on 2026-04-15. Handover §5 step 8 calls for POST /api/tags/attach-to-campaigns at deploy time. `ebClient.attachTagsToCampaigns` was implemented in Phase 1 uncommitted work (src/lib/emailbison/client.ts). Phase 3 wiring blocked: no Workspace field holds the tag IDs to attach. Verified via grep of prisma/schema.prisma — only `emailBisonCampaignId` (Campaign + CampaignDeploy) and `emailBisonSenderId` (Sender) exist; no `ebTagIds`, `emailBisonTagIds`, `defaultEbTags`, `workspaceEbTags`, or equivalent on Workspace. Phase 3 preserved Step 8 as documented no-op (email-adapter.ts:432-444) with clear comment. Fix: (1) add `Workspace.ebTagIds Int[]?` or `Workspace.ebTagIds Json?` (array of ints per EB /api/tags shape); (2) add admin UI in workspace settings to set it; (3) wire into email-adapter.ts Step 8 via ebClient.attachTagsToCampaigns({ tagIds: workspace.ebTagIds, campaignIds: [ebCampaignId] }); (4) add test coverage for the wired step. Not a blocker for the 2026-04-15 launches (tags are non-critical — campaigns deploy without them, just lose EB-side tag-based reporting categorisation). Phase 4 can GO without this fix.

items.title: createSequenceStep wire-format + v1.1 endpoint migration — Phase 6a canary root cause
items.notes: Phase 6a canary failed 422 on email deploy with identical error string to pre-rebuild 2026-04-15 failure (CampaignDeploy cmo00nd6g000pp8j53qk6h1ky). items.title: Campaign.status optimistic rollback gap on deploy failure
items.notes: QA F4. src/lib/campaigns/deploy-campaign.ts:130-158 flips Campaign.status approved→deployed OPTIMISTICALLY before async Trigger.dev task runs. src/lib/campaigns/deploy.ts:174-197 executeDeploy catch-block only updates CampaignDeploy row — never rolls Campaign.status back, never clears deployedAt, never clears emailBisonCampaignId. Phase 5.5 commit 184db22c was a manual SQL fix for this exact drift but the systemic fix never landed. Every failed deploy now leaves zombie Campaign state (status=deployed, ebId=populated, deployedAt=non-null, but CampaignDeploy.status=failed in sibling table). Phase 6a canary reproduced this exactly. Fix: executeDeploy catch block must atomically revert Campaign.status→approved AND clear emailBisonCampaignId AND clear deployedAt on terminal failure. Must also handle the retry case (do not rollback if another CampaignDeploy row is still pending).

items.title: Trigger.dev retry semantics + serial-retry orphan creation
items.notes: QA F3+F8. trigger/campaign-deploy.ts:14-19 has retry.maxAttempts=2. Phase 6a canary produced 2 EB drafts (78+79, 17s apart) — suggests Trigger.dev fired a retry after Step 3 422, re-entering deploy.ts with state that re-created EB campaign instead of reusing preExistingEbId. Compensating-tx did NOT delete orphan EB 78. The concurrent-race test (email-adapter-race.test.ts) exercises P2002 but NOT the serial-retry path. Need: (a) retry-idempotency integration test reproducing Phase 6a dual-orphan, (b) root-cause whether Trigger.dev retry bypasses the preExistingEbId reuse branch in email-adapter.ts, (c) decide whether createCampaign retries should be guarded by a separate idempotency key. Do NOT start fixes until BL-074 lands — they compound. [CLOSED 2026-04-16T13:55:00Z Phase 6.5b Bundle C] Root cause: Phase 6a dual-orphan was most plausibly a withRetry-induced duplicate at email-adapter.ts:243 (withRetry wraps createCampaign with 3-attempt 1s/5s/15s backoff; if EB creates the campaign but client doesn't see the 2xx, withRetry's next attempt creates ANOTHER draft — the 17s gap between EB 78 and EB 79 matches the 15s withRetry delay). Trigger.dev's retry then reused correctly (preExistingEbId=79). Fix: Option α+γ combined. (1) Removed withRetry wrapping createCampaign at email-adapter.ts:243 — createCampaign is non-idempotent so client-side retry is unsafe; transient failures now fall through to Trigger.dev task-level retry which uses the idempotent reuse branch. (2) Reordered Step 2 writes: CampaignDeploy.emailBisonCampaignId persisted FIRST (retry anchor), Campaign second — closes the window where process death between writes could lose the anchor. (3) executeDeploy entry now detects Trigger.dev retry via CampaignDeploy.status='failed' + emailBisonCampaignId != null, restores Campaign state (status→deployed, ebId from CampaignDeploy, deployedAt→now) in an atomic tx, flips CampaignDeploy 'failed'→'running' with error/completedAt cleared, then proceeds past the status guard. (4) Defensive guard throws on any other entry status (complete, running, or failed-without-anchor) to prevent silent duplicate creates. Coordinated with Bundle B (BL-075): rollback still fires on terminal failure (clears Campaign state), retry restore brings it back via anchor — net effect is that each attempt sees Campaign 'deployed' with the reusable EB ID, and terminal non-retry failure leaves clean rolled-back state. New 4 retry integration tests at src/lib/campaigns/__tests__/deploy-rollback.test.ts (retry-happy, retry-still-fails, defensive-guard-complete, defensive-guard-no-anchor) reproduce the Phase 6a scenario and assert ONE createCampaign call across TWO executeDeploy invocations with the same deployId. Bundle B QA F1 (retry-after-rollback CRITICAL): RESOLVED by retry-entry restore. F2 (state-machine transition failed→running HIGH): RESOLVED by guarded updateMany (status='failed' guard) with error/completedAt cleared. F3 (zero retry-test coverage HIGH): RESOLVED by 4 new retry test cases. Commit: pending. BL-077 (EB 78 cleanup) stays open. BL-076 closed.
