items.title: EmailBisonClient error-class mismatch: EmailBisonApiError vs EmailBisonError
items.notes: Phase 6a-rollback QA F1. client.getCampaign() throws TWO error classes: (a) EmailBisonApiError (src/lib/emailbison/client.ts:44-66) has .status + .isRecordNotFound getter when HTTP returns non-2xx, OR (b) EmailBisonError from src/lib/emailbison/types.ts:336 with code='CAMPAIGN_NOT_FOUND', statusCode=404 (thrown at client.ts:334-339 when EB returns 200 with empty/missing data). EB's DELETE is async per docs/emailbison-dedi-api-reference.md:572-589 — GET during the cleanup window returns 200-with-empty-data, triggering EmailBisonError (not EmailBisonApiError). Callers that narrow to `e as EmailBisonApiError` and check `err.isRecordNotFound || err.status === 404` silently mishandle EmailBisonError (status=undefined, isRecordNotFound=undefined → rethrow). Surfaced in _phase-6a-rollback-email-canary.ts:150-161 as a spurious failure that misdiagnosed as 'transient fetch retry race'. Fix: (a) introduce a shared helper `isNotFoundError(e): boolean` covering BOTH classes, (b) grep all callers of getCampaign/deleteCampaign for the pattern `isRecordNotFound || .status === 404` and refactor, (c) add unit test mocking BOTH error paths. Blocks clean BL-077 execution. [CLOSED 2026-04-16T10:25:00Z Phase 6.5b Bundle D] isNotFoundError helper added at src/lib/emailbison/errors.ts; migrated 1 tracked-source caller in src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts (Step 1 manual-delete guard — the canonical site from BL-078 brief); unit tests cover both error classes (EmailBisonApiError status=404/record_not_found/500/400, EmailBisonError CAMPAIGN_NOT_FOUND + statusCode=404-only + non-404) + non-Error inputs (null/undefined/string/plain object/symbol/number/plain Error) — 10 cases total. Maintenance script scripts/maintenance/_phase-6a-rollback-email-canary.ts intentionally left untracked per preservation rule — BL-077 (future orphan cleanup) should import isNotFoundError from the new export when authored. Internal EmailBisonClient 404→null narrows (client.ts:601/993/1011 for schedule + blacklisted-domain + blacklisted-email) intentionally left untouched — circular-dep risk (errors.ts imports EmailBisonApiError FROM client.ts, so client.ts can't import isNotFoundError FROM errors.ts without refactoring the class location); also these internal methods only ever see EmailBisonApiError (never throw EmailBisonError themselves), so functional risk is zero. Admin/portal inbox action routes (src/app/api/{admin,portal}/inbox/email/actions/route.ts) intentionally left untouched — their catch structure uses narrowed EmailBisonApiError.{status,body} for logging + HTTP response mapping BEYOND the 404 branch; mechanical helper replacement would lose the narrowed type. These routes ARE missing 404-handling on the EmailBisonError class (latent inconsistency) but fixing requires restructuring, not just helper adoption — deferred to a future ticket. Gates: tsc clean, eslint no-restricted-imports=0, vitest 842 pass (832 baseline + 10 new) / 37 pre-existing fail / 1 todo / 0 new fail.

items.title: Step-1 failure retries silently dropped by Bundle C defensive guard
items.notes:
Phase 6.5b Bundle C QA F1. src/lib/campaigns/deploy.ts:126-180 new retry-entry logic detects Trigger.dev retry via CampaignDeploy.status='failed' AND CampaignDeploy.emailBisonCampaignId != null; only then does it restore Campaign state and flip CampaignDeploy 'failed'→'running' to proceed past the status guard. The ELSE branch (status='failed' AND anchor IS null — meaning Step 1 failed BEFORE email-adapter's Step 2 anchor write) now falls through to a defensive throw. This is a regression vs pre-Bundle-B behaviour: a transient Step 1 failure (network timeout pre-EB-create, auth token rotation mid-flight, Zod parse drift on EB createCampaign response, getCampaign returning null on the reuse-branch verification) that USED to succeed on Trigger.dev's second attempt now silently dead-ends. Operator workaround: manually flip CampaignDeploy.status back to 'pending' and re-invoke. Fix options: (a) broaden retry detection to ALSO cover status='failed' AND anchor=null (treat as 'retry the whole deploy from scratch'), (b) differentiate pre-anchor failure errors (rethrow to Trigger.dev without flipping CampaignDeploy to 'failed', so retry sees 'running' and passes the guard), (c) add an explicit CampaignDeploy.failureStage field so retry logic can distinguish pre-Step-2 from post-Step-2 failures and route accordingly. Option (a) is simplest; (c) most explicit. Do NOT ship Phase 6.5c with this gap if BL-079 might fire in the 17-campaign rollout — Step 1 transient failures are realistic under EB load.

[2026-04-17T10:30:00Z CLOSED] Approach (a) implemented: broadened retry detection in src/lib/campaigns/deploy.ts entry-branch. Previous defensive throw on (status='failed', anchor=null) replaced with a PRE-ANCHOR retry path that (1) advances Campaign 'approved'→'deployed' via updateMany guarded on status='approved' AND emailBisonCampaignId=null (idempotent with Bundle B's post-rollback shape), (2) flips CampaignDeploy 'failed'→'running' with error/completedAt cleared, (3) lets Step 1 take the fresh-create path (preExistingEbId stays null because we have nothing to restore). Safety: no EB resource exists from the failed first attempt (anchor=null proves it), so no duplicate EB campaign risk. Added src/lib/campaigns/__tests__/deploy-rollback.test.ts "BL-079 pre-anchor retry re-entry" describe block with 6 cases covering network TypeError, auth 401, Zod UNEXPECTED_RESPONSE, transient 5xx, hard 4xx (retry enters cleanly but surfaces deterministic error), and concurrent-mutation idempotency. Old defensive-guard test on (failed+null) was replaced with the new positive-path tests plus a status='running' defensive guard test. Deploy.ts entry-comment updated to document all three legal states. Option (a) chosen over (b) because (b) requires adapter-level error classification and broader change surface; option (c) deferred pending schema migration. Gates: tsc clean, vitest 16/16 pass on deploy-rollback.test.ts. Overlap with BL-091: BL-091 calls for an integration test with REAL withRetry + mock ebClient; BL-079 tests are ORTHOGONAL (they mock the adapter entirely to isolate the retry state-machine). BL-091 remains open as separate work. Commit SHA captured in completion report.

items.title: Bundle C retry-happy test does not actually assert single createCampaign across retry
items.notes: Phase 6.5b Bundle C QA F2. src/lib/campaigns/__tests__/deploy-rollback.test.ts lines 478-614 'retry-happy' case mocks the EmailAdapter wholesale — the actual `email-adapter.ts` Step 1 preExistingEbId reuse branch is never exercised, so the test cannot prove that a Trigger.dev retry takes the reuse path rather than calling createCampaign a second time. The claim 'SINGLE createCampaign call across 2 executeDeploy invocations' is asserted only at the mock-level, which is already 1 because the adapter is mocked as a no-op on retry. The dual-orphan regression gate is in name only. Fix: write an integration-level test that mocks ebClient at the HTTP boundary (not the adapter) and invokes executeDeploy end-to-end TWICE with the same deployId + a transient adapter-internal failure on the first call. Assert ebClient.createCampaign is called exactly ONCE across both attempts. Either add to deploy-rollback.test.ts or as a new file trigger/__tests__/campaign-deploy-retry-e2e.test.ts. Without this, the Phase 6a dual-orphan scenario has no actual regression test despite the Bundle C commit claiming one.

items.title: Bundle C commit forensic math is incorrect (15s delay never fires with maxRetries=3)
items.notes: Phase 6.5b Bundle C QA F3 (info correction, non-functional). Bundle C commit message + decisions.md entry + architecture.md entry all claim the EB 78 → EB 79 17s gap matches withRetry's '15s final delay'. src/lib/utils/retry.ts:11-29 shows default delays [1000, 5000, 15000] with maxRetries=3, meaning: attempt 1 runs immediately, delay 1s, attempt 2 runs, delay 5s, attempt 3 runs, delay 15s THEN LOOP EXITS (the 15s delay is post-attempt-3 with nothing after it — never fires as a between-attempts gap). The actual between-attempts gaps are 1s (between 1-2) and 5s (between 2-3). 17s is more consistent with a long first-attempt latency (EB campaign-create IS a heavy operation — can take 5-10s under load) + 5s retry delay + second-attempt latency ≈ 17s total elapsed. Fix is STILL correct on general grounds (non-idempotent POST + client-layer retry = always unsafe regardless of timing) — but the specific arithmetic in the forensic claim is wrong and should be corrected in memory to avoid future agents citing false evidence. Action: amend architecture.md 'sibling-row retry-anchor' entry + decisions.md Phase 6.5b Bundle C entry to replace the '15s delay matches 17s gap' claim with the accurate 'client-side retry of a non-idempotent POST is always unsafe; withRetry's default behaviour of retrying 5xx and transient errors made duplicate EB creates inevitable under EB latency variance.'

items.title: Keepalive health-update route does not co-write sessionStatus when flipping healthStatus='session_expired'
items.notes: Phase 6a-retry QA F2. Surfaced when the LinkedIn canary preflight halted: sender cmmxon6vi0001p85cr1g1l124 (Daniel, daniel@1210solutions.co.uk, workspaceSlug=1210-solutions, channel='both') displayed the contradictory state sessionStatus='active' + healthStatus='session_expired'. Root cause: worker/src/keepalive.ts:146 calls api.updateSenderHealth(senderId, 'session_expired') when the LinkedIn keepalive endpoint returns non-200; that handler at src/app/api/linkedin/senders/[id]/health/route.ts:39-47 updates Sender.healthStatus ONLY (does not co-write sessionStatus). Two other LinkedIn recovery paths DO co-write both fields correctly: src/lib/linkedin/session-refresh.ts:58-64 (atomic update) and src/app/api/extension/senders/[id]/expiry/route.ts:47-64 (atomic update). The inconsistency produces a split-brain where app-layer desired state (sessionStatus='active') disagrees with observed health (healthStatus='session_expired'). Phase 6a-preflight gate-12 at scripts/maintenance/_phase6a-preflight.ts:222-226 filters on sessionStatus==='active' && healthStatus IN ('healthy','warning'), so the split-brain correctly fails the gate — but the preflight halt happens much later than the keepalive write, so operators see a 'canary blocked' symptom without immediate visibility of the underlying keepalive failure. Auto-recovery exists at src/lib/linkedin/health-check.ts:93-127 (Step 3.5, runs 6am daily) — this paper-overs the contradiction but doesn't fix the write gap. Fix: in the PATCH handler at src/app/api/linkedin/senders/[id]/health/route.ts, when healthStatus transitions to 'session_expired', atomically co-write sessionStatus='expired' in the same prisma.sender.update call. Also audit other health-write call sites for the same co-write-or-drop pattern (admin reactivate at src/app/api/senders/[id]/reactivate/route.ts flips healthStatus='healthy' without refreshing sessionData — the INVERSE of this bug, and per Phase 6a-retry QA F4 creates a preflight false-positive trap where a 'recovered' sender passes gate-12 but still has a broken cookie). Not a Phase 6a-retry blocker (PM will resolve Daniel's session via extension re-auth), but this preflight halt will keep recurring on 1210-solutions and other workspaces until the co-write fix lands. Add a unit test asserting that updateSenderHealth('session_expired') also sets sessionStatus='expired'.

items.title: Writer audit + canonical emailSequence shape enforcement
items.notes:
Investigation ticket filed after BL-083 repair. 5 1210 Solutions email campaigns (all created 2026-03-31) had Campaign.emailSequence stored with LinkedIn-shape keys: {stepNumber, channel, type, subject, subjectB, body, delayDays, notes}. The canonical shape expected by src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:101-111 (StoredEmailSequenceStepSchema) + the writer input schema at src/lib/agents/writer.ts:209-221 is {position, subjectLine, subjectVariantB?, body, delayDays, notes?}. The shape mismatch blocked the deploy canary at Step 2 with a BL-068 zod error. One campaign (Healthcare cmneqhwo50001p843r5hmsul3) was already in canonical shape, suggesting the drift was inconsistent — some writes use canonical, some use LinkedIn-shape.

Investigation findings:

1. Current writer code path is CLEAN: src/lib/agents/writer.ts:209-221 declares saveCampaignSequence input schema with {position, subjectLine, subjectVariantB?, body, delayDays, notes?} and passes it straight into saveCampaignSequences (src/lib/campaigns/operations.ts:756-764) which JSON.stringifies without validation. So the current writer path cannot produce LinkedIn-shape keys.

2. saveCampaignSequences (src/lib/campaigns/operations.ts:756) accepts `unknown[]` and JSON.stringifies with no zod parse. This is the permissive boundary — any caller (not just writer) can write arbitrary shape. Defense-in-depth gap.

3. Current CLI wrappers are CLEAN: scripts/cli/save-sequence.ts + scripts/cli/save-draft.ts both delegate to writerTools.saveCampaignSequence.execute with the zod schema. Current dist build from 2026-04-15 11:28 has the canonical `position: z.number()` schema.

4. Legacy source: scripts/maintenance/_apply_greetings_2026_04_15.ts:24-33 declares a LOCAL `TenStep` type with {stepNumber, channel, type, subject, subjectB, body, delayDays, notes} — this reflects the shape found in 1210's rows. Script ran 2026-04-15 and would have PRESERVED whatever shape was there (it loads via prisma.campaign.findUniqueOrThrow, mutates body fields only, writes back via saveCampaignSequences). It did not CAUSE the drift but it confirms the LinkedIn shape existed pre-2026-04-15.

5. The 5 campaigns were created 2026-03-31 (before the writer zod schema migration to canonical). Historical writer code or agent SDK prompting may have emitted {stepNumber, channel, type, subject, subjectB} via an older schema, and those rows have never been re-saved since.

Recommended next steps:
- Add zod .parse() guard in saveCampaignSequences (src/lib/campaigns/operations.ts:756-764) before JSON.stringify — fail loud on non-canonical writes.
- Write a one-shot audit script scripts/maintenance/_bl084_emailsequence_shape_audit.ts that iterates ALL Campaign rows with non-null emailSequence, JSON.parses, attempts StoredEmailSequenceStepSchema.parse, and lists every row that fails. Should be run once to confirm no other workspaces have silent drift.
- Git-blame scripts/maintenance/_apply_greetings_2026_04_15.ts + any prior writer.ts revisions touching Campaign.emailSequence literals to find when the LinkedIn shape was emitted. Fix scope: enforcement only — historical rows already repaired by BL-083.
- Consider adding a post-parse zod guard in formatCampaignDetail (src/lib/campaigns/operations.ts:221) as well so the READ boundary fails loud on drift — today parseJsonArray returns raw parsed JSON without shape validation, so the drift only surfaces at deploy time.

Bounded scope — no writer code changes in this phase. Filed during BL-083 remediation.

[2026-04-16 QA follow-through per Finding 5]
- Run git log --before=2026-04-01 --after=2026-03-25 -p -- src/lib/agents/writer.ts src/lib/campaigns/operations.ts to identify source commit (not done in phase).
- Split into sub-tasks: (1) zod.parse() guard in saveCampaignSequences (P1, closes ticket), (2) one-shot _bl084_emailsequence_shape_audit.ts dry-run script (P1), (3) formatCampaignDetail read-boundary guard (P2 defense-in-depth).
- BLOCKED BY BL-085: adapter-level empty-subject handling must land first, or guard will reject legitimate reply-in-thread writes.

items.title: email-adapter reply-in-thread support + EB 81 orphan cleanup
items.notes:
BLOCKING 1210 email deploys. Identified via canary Run E 2026-04-16 (after BL-083 data repair).

ROOT CAUSE: email-adapter.ts:405-412 + emailbison/client.ts:380 chain produces 'email_subject: ""' on reply-in-thread follow-up steps. EB v1.1 batch sequence-steps endpoint rejects with 422: 'The sequence_steps.1.email_subject field is required.'

TRIGGER: any emailSequence step with position>1 and subjectLine empty/null (intentional reply-in-thread marker).

IMPACT: canary cmneqixpv Run E failed at Step 3. Auto-rollback (BL-075) fired correctly. Created NEW ORPHAN EB 81 (status=draft, 0 leads, 0 steps) in 1210-solutions workspace. Same class as EB 78/80 (BL-077).

FIX OPTIONS (per QA Finding 1):
  (a) Adapter substitutes fallback subject when subjectLine empty on position>1: 'Re: ' + firstStepSubject (email thread convention)
  (b) Check docs/emailbison-dedi-api-reference.md for EB v1.1 'inherit subject' / 'reply_in_thread' flag on sequence-steps — thread that through instead of faking subject
  (c) Reject at deploy pre-flight if any follow-up step has empty subject — force writer/content fix upstream

---

[2026-04-16 CASCADE UPDATE] After the empty-subject fix (commit 496d2d9c) landed, canary Run F on 2026-04-16 16:xx exposed a NEW compound bug at Step 3:

1. createSequenceSteps Zod schema (client.ts) rejects EB v1.1 actual 200 response. Docs don't document v1.1 response shape — original schema was guessed from v1 spike notes.
2. email-adapter.ts:475 wrapped createSequenceSteps in withRetry — Zod throws → withRetry retries → EB inserts another full batch. 3 retries = 3x duplicate steps in EB. Same BL-076 amplifier class (BL-076 fix only removed withRetry from createCampaign, not createSequenceSteps).

Evidence: CampaignDeploy cmo1ig1yf status=failed, emailError='[step:3] EmailBison error UNEXPECTED_RESPONSE (200)', emailBisonCampaignId=82. EB 82 had 9 sequence steps (3x duplicate from 3 withRetry attempts).

FIX LANDED (commit df0ed71d, 2026-04-16):
  - Fix A: client.ts createSequenceSteps — on HTTP 200 + Zod fail, warn log (raw first 500 chars + Zod error) and return [] instead of throwing. Zod schema preserved for shape expectation.
  - Fix B: email-adapter.ts:475 — removed withRetry wrapper. Full withRetry audit matrix in commit body (only L475 removed, others KEEP per idempotency review).
  - Fix C (ad-hoc cleanup, not in commit): Deleted orphan EB 82 + also found and deleted orphan EB 81 (pre-cascade artifact). 1210-solutions EB workspace now clean (0 campaigns).
  - Fix D: 14 tests in client-sequence-steps.test.ts including tolerant-parse scenarios + regression for canary failure mode. All pass. npx tsc --noEmit clean.

FIX F result (stage deploy retry): Step 3 now clean — single POST, Zod failed (as expected with v1.1 response), warn logged with full raw body, tolerant parse returned [], EB had exactly 3 sequence steps (no duplicates). CONFIRMED the amplifier is neutralized.

HOWEVER Run F exposed a NEW Step 5 bug filed as BL-087 (createSchedule 422 save_as_template required). BL-075 auto-rollback fired correctly, EB 83 cleaned up same session, canary cmneqixpv restored to approved state.

QA findings on the df0ed71d commit (see incidents.md lines 235-251) flagged two HIGH issues:
  F1: withRetry matrix reasoning incorrect — outer withRetry retries ALL errors regardless of EmailBisonClient internal retry set. Other non-idempotent POSTs (createSchedule, createLead, attachLeadsToCampaign, attachSenderEmails) still have latent amplifier risk. Filed as BL-086.
  F2: createLead/attachLeadsToCampaign dedupe assumptions are UNDOCUMENTED in EB docs. Per EB reference line 1458 createLead email 'Must be unique' implies 422 on duplicate, NOT silent dedupe. Covered by BL-086.

REMAINING WORK on BL-085 itself: blocked by BL-087 (Step 5 schedule 422) before canary can complete end-to-end deploy. Once BL-087 is fixed, re-run stage deploy. If clean end-to-end, close BL-085.

[2026-04-16T19:30:00Z FIXES LANDED commit ca2fe6a3 (BL-086+BL-087); BL-085 STAYS in_progress] Stage-deploy Run G (2026-04-16T15:01) outcome:
  - Step 1-3: clean (createSequenceSteps tolerant-parse from df0ed71d held; single POST; 3 sequence steps in EB)
  - Step 4 (createLead): FAILED with EB 422 'The email has already been taken.' Single POST attempt — confirms BL-086 amplifier neutralization works. Auto-rollback (BL-075) fired correctly: Campaign cmneqixpv reverted to status=approved + emailBisonCampaignId=null + deployedAt=null. CampaignDeploy cmo1lxql70001zxo4t9e8zg92 marked status=failed.
  - Orphan EB 84 deleted same session via scripts/maintenance/_bl087-cleanup-eb84.ts. 1210-solutions EB workspace clean (0 campaigns).

NEW BUG SURFACED at Step 4: createLead is non-idempotent across EB workspace lead store. Per EB docs line 1458 'email Must be unique' — and confirmed live: lead lorraine.thomas@metzger.co.uk was created at 2026-04-16T13:42:02 (likely Run F before EB campaign deletion). EB deletes the campaign but PRESERVES the workspace-scoped leads. Subsequent createLead on the same email returns 422 immediately. Filed as BL-088.

BL-085 stays in_progress until end-to-end Steps 1-8 pass clean. Blocked by BL-088.

[2026-04-16T22:00:00Z CLOSED] BL-085 cascade fully unblocked. Step 3 tolerant-parse fix from commit df0ed71d was implicitly live-validated when canary cmneqixpv reached Step 8 via BL-088 landing — Step 3 now passes cleanly, no duplicate sequence_steps, warn not triggered (real response matched schema). BL-086 (status-aware withRetry) + BL-087 (save_as_template required) + BL-088 (createLead upsert) cascade complete. Closed.

items.title: Audit remaining withRetry wraps on non-idempotent EB POSTs
items.notes:
Filed from QA findings F1+F2 on commit df0ed71d (BL-085 cascade fix). QA identified that the withRetry audit matrix used by the BL-085 Fix B reasoning is flawed: the outer withRetry() in src/lib/utils/retry.ts:11-29 catches ALL thrown errors and retries 3x with [1000ms, 5000ms, 15000ms] delays, regardless of the INNER EmailBisonClient.RETRYABLE_STATUSES set {429,500,502,503,504} at client.ts:85.

Impact: when any EB POST throws on a non-retryable status (e.g. 422), withRetry burns ~21s and fires 3 POSTs before giving up. For non-idempotent POSTs this can produce duplicates (same class as the BL-085 Step 3 amplifier just removed).

AFFECTED CALL SITES in src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:
  - Line 544 createLead (POST). EB docs line 1458: email 'Must be unique' — implies 422 on duplicate, not silent dedupe. Retry will 3x the 422 cost. Could also 422-retry if EB asynchronously accepts-then-fails on a subsequent call.
  - Line 582 attachLeadsToCampaign (POST). Dedupe NOT documented in EB reference (lines 424-441).
  - Line 610 createSchedule reuse-fallback POST. Single schedule per campaign — duplicate likely 422. withRetry 3x on 422 is wasted latency.
  - Line 615 createSchedule fresh POST. Same as 610.
  - Line 646 attachSenderEmails (POST). Dedupe assumed but not documented.

FIX OPTIONS:
  (a) Remove withRetry from each of these POSTs (consistent with BL-076 + BL-085 pattern for non-idempotent POSTs). If transient 5xx happens, surface up to auto-rollback. Simple and conservative.
  (b) Make withRetry status-aware: inspect EmailBisonApiError.status and skip retry for non-retryable statuses (mirror the inner client retry set). More sophisticated but preserves resilience on true transient failures.
  (c) Per-call audit — some (e.g. createLead) may be safe to retry if we confirm EB dedupe empirically; others (createSchedule POST on singleton) get withRetry removed.

Preferred: (b) to avoid re-litigating every POST, + controlled probe against a throwaway EB campaign to document actual dedupe behavior for createLead / attachLeadsToCampaign / attachSenderEmails (update docs/emailbison-dedi-api-reference.md and email-adapter.ts comments with findings).

QA trail: .monty/memory/incidents.md lines 235-251. PRIORITY: blocks confident ship of BL-087 (Step 5 save_as_template fix) and any future BL-085-class amplifier discovery.

[2026-04-16T19:30:00Z RESOLVED commit ca2fe6a3] Approach (b) implemented: status-aware withRetry. Predicate isRetryableError(err) extracted as exported helper for testability. Retry rules:
  - EmailBisonApiError with status in {429,500,502,503,504} -> retry (mirrors EmailBisonClient.RETRYABLE_STATUSES at client.ts:85)
  - EmailBisonApiError with any other status (4xx non-429) -> NO retry (deterministic server rejection)
  - EmailBisonError (Zod / UNEXPECTED_RESPONSE / business semantics) -> NO retry
  - TypeError (network/fetch failures) -> retry
  - Anything else -> NO retry (defensive default)

Tests: src/lib/utils/__tests__/retry-status-aware.test.ts (26 cases) covers the predicate matrix + integrated withRetry call counts. BL-086 amplifier neutralization confirmed live by canary stage-deploy 2026-04-16T15:01: Step 4 createLead hit 422 'email already taken' and threw IMMEDIATELY (single POST, no 21s loop, no duplicate side-effects). Auto-rollback fired correctly. Closed.

items.title: EB v1.1 createSchedule 422 — save_as_template field required
items.notes:
BLOCKING 1210 email canary end-to-end. Surfaced during BL-085 cascade Fix F stage-deploy retry 2026-04-16 (on EB 83, subsequently cleaned up).

ROOT CAUSE: EB v1.1 contract change for POST /campaigns/{id}/schedule now requires a `save_as_template` field in the body. DEFAULT_SCHEDULE at src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:122-133 omits it on the fresh-deploy path (branch at ~L615). Returns 422 'The save as template field is required.'

SYMPTOM: CampaignDeploy `cmo1j39vx` status=failed, emailError='[step:5] 422 save_as_template required', emailBisonCampaignId=83 (deleted as part of session cleanup).

FIX: add `save_as_template: false` to DEFAULT_SCHEDULE (default false matches intent — we're not templating per-campaign schedules). Add test: email-adapter schedule-step with fresh campaign path → wire format includes save_as_template. Also verify the reuse-fallback POST path (email-adapter.ts:610) includes the same field.

INTERACTS WITH BL-086: if withRetry stays on the createSchedule POST (as in current code), a 422 here will waste ~21s on 3 retries before surfacing. Landing BL-086 (withRetry removal or status-aware retry) alongside this fix avoids the amplifier.

NICE-TO-HAVE: discover other EB v1.1 schedule-body field drift by probing GET /campaigns/{id}/schedule on an existing active EB campaign and comparing to DEFAULT_SCHEDULE keys.

LATENT RISK DISCOVERED SAME SESSION (not a blocker, not filed as separate ticket yet): getSequenceSteps GET response shape under v1.1 is `{data: {sequence_id, sequence_steps: [...]}}` nested, NOT `{data: [...]}` flat. Current src/lib/emailbison/client.ts getSequenceSteps Zod schema will fail on reuse path (email-adapter.ts:401 — only hit when preExistingEbId != null). Fresh-deploy path skips this GET so Fix F didn't exercise it. If the BL-085 fix inspired a tolerant-parse pattern, consider extending the tolerant-parse approach to getSequenceSteps OR broadening the schema union to handle both nested and flat. Not in scope for BL-087; file separately if/when a reuse-path deploy is attempted.

[2026-04-16T19:30:00Z RESOLVED commit ca2fe6a3] DEFAULT_SCHEDULE in src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts now includes save_as_template:false; the constant flows through both fresh POST (~L615) and reuse PUT (~L603) paths. Removed the inline override at the updateSchedule call site since the constant supplies it. Updated src/__tests__/email-adapter-deploy.test.ts happy-path assertion to expect the new field. Doc comment in email-adapter.ts flags the EB v1.1 spec drift (docs still mark save_as_template optional but live API requires it on POST).

Verification: canary stage-deploy 2026-04-16T15:01 advanced past the prior Step 5 failure point and instead failed at Step 4 (createLead 422 — different bug, BL-088). Step 5 fix is verifiable by absence of the [step:5] save_as_template error in the new failure mode. Closed.

NICE-TO-HAVE not done in this commit: getSequenceSteps response shape probe. Filed deferred under BL-085 notes; reuse-path deploys would need it.

items.title: createLead 422 on duplicate email — switch to upsert via /api/leads/create-or-update
items.notes:
BLOCKING 1210 email canary end-to-end. Surfaced during BL-085 cascade Fix G stage-deploy retry 2026-04-16T15:01 (canary cmneqixpv0001p8710bov1fga, EB 84 — subsequently cleaned up).

ROOT CAUSE: src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:558 calls EmailBisonClient.createLead() in the per-lead loop. The comment at L521 claims 'EB's createLead is idempotent by email on the lead side' — this assumption is FALSE. Per EB docs line 1458 (POST /api/leads): 'email - Must be unique and in a valid email format.' The live API returns 422 'The email has already been taken.' on a duplicate.

WHY THIS DIDN'T SHOW UP IN PRIOR CANARY RUNS: lead store in EmailBison is WORKSPACE-SCOPED, not campaign-scoped. Deleting an EB campaign (e.g. EB 78/80/81/82/83 cleanups) does NOT delete the leads that were created during that campaign's deploy attempt. They persist in the workspace lead list. Run F (2026-04-16T13:42) successfully created at least 44 leads in the 1210-solutions workspace before the schedule failure. Run G's createLead loop on the same TargetList immediately 422'd on the first lead (lorraine.thomas@metzger.co.uk, EB lead id 22148, created_at=2026-04-16T13:42:02).

PROOF: scripts/maintenance/_bl087-check-eb-state.ts probed the EB lead store and confirmed lorraine.thomas@metzger.co.uk exists with id=22148 from the prior run. CampaignDeploy cmo1lxql70001zxo4t9e8zg92 emailError='[step:4] 422 The email has already been taken.'

AMPLIFIER STATUS: BL-086 (status-aware withRetry) is LANDED, so the 422 surfaces immediately on the first POST attempt — no 21s amplifier loop, no extra duplicate side-effects beyond the one row that already exists. Confirms the amplifier neutralization works correctly under a real 422.

FIX OPTIONS:
  (a) Switch the per-lead createLead to POST /api/leads/create-or-update/multiple (docs line 1595) which is the documented upsert variant. Bulk-friendly (limit 500/request), supports existing_lead_behavior='patch' to preserve existing data while updating fields we send. Net effect: each createLead call becomes idempotent, retries are safe, prior-run debris no longer blocks fresh runs.
  (b) Catch the 422 in email-adapter, parse error.errors.email, look up the existing lead by email via GET /api/leads?search={email}, reuse that lead.id for attachLeadsToCampaign. Lower blast radius (no client method change) but burns extra round-trips.
  (c) Pre-flight: GET /api/leads?search={email} for every lead BEFORE createLead, branch to update vs create. Even more round-trips than (b).

PREFERRED: (a). The bulk upsert endpoint matches our pattern (we already write workspace-scoped leads in batches), is documented as the canonical re-upload-without-loss approach (docs line 1527 'Instead of deleting, simply re-upload the leads. We'll update the records in place'), and removes the entire class of cross-run lead duplication bugs. Implementation: add EmailBisonClient.createOrUpdateLeadsMultiple() that POSTs to /api/leads/create-or-update/multiple with existing_lead_behavior='patch' and an array of lead bodies; replace the per-lead loop in email-adapter.ts:538-573 with a single batch call. Returns array of {id, email, ...} per lead — capture the IDs the same way createdLeadIds.push currently does. Update the L521 comment to remove the wrong idempotency claim and reference the new endpoint.

INTERACTS WITH:
  - BL-086 (status-aware withRetry, landed) — keeps the new bulk POST safe to wrap in withRetry only on transient 5xx; 422 still surfaces immediately.
  - BL-085 (cascade) — closing BL-088 should unblock the end-to-end canary deploy and let BL-085 close.
  - BL-077 (orphan EB cleanup ticket, closed) — adds a follow-up: even after closing BL-088, prior-run leads still sit in the 1210-solutions workspace lead list. Not a blocker (the upsert handles them) but worth noting that the 'workspace lead history' is not reset when canary attempts are retried.

NICE-TO-HAVE: the same upsert pattern likely applies to attachLeadsToCampaign and attachSenderEmails dedupe assumptions (BL-086 F2 callouts). After landing BL-088, audit those two POSTs against EB docs and live behavior to confirm whether they're truly idempotent or just haven't 422'd yet.

Cleanup state at filing: EB 84 deleted; canary cmneqixpv reverted via BL-075 rollback (status=approved, ebId=null); 1210-solutions EB workspace = 0 campaigns. Workspace lead store NOT cleaned (44+ leads from Run F still present — that's the bug surface BL-088 fixes).

[2026-04-16T22:00:00Z RESOLVED commit 33a9c3c4] Approach (a) implemented: switched email-adapter Step 4 per-lead createLead loop to batch createOrUpdateLeadsMultiple hitting /api/leads/create-or-update/multiple with existing_lead_behavior=patch. Tolerant Zod parse per BL-085 discipline, [BL-088] warn prefix on drift. Tests: src/lib/emailbison/__tests__/client-create-or-update-leads.test.ts (12 cases) + src/lib/channels/__tests__/email-adapter-step4-upsert.test.ts (5 cases). Canary stage-deploy 2026-04-16T15:31 LIVE-VALIDATED: cmneqixpv → status=deployed, emailBisonCampaignId=85, 44 retained workspace leads upserted with ZERO 422s, Step 8 reached, skipResume branch fired correctly, deployedAt set, emailError="STAGED — resume pending PM review". EB 85 awaits PM cleanup authorization. Closed.

items.title: createOrUpdateLeadsMultiple: implement 500-lead chunking
items.notes: QA F2 on commit 33a9c3c4. src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts Step 4 sends ALL eligible leads in one batch POST to /api/leads/create-or-update/multiple. EB docs line 1599 caps at 500 leads/request. Canary ran 44 leads so boundary was not exercised. A workspace with 501+ eligible leads will hit an undocumented failure mode (probable EB 422 or silent truncation). TODO comment is present at email-adapter.ts:584-587 but not implemented. Fix: chunk eligibleLeads into slices of 500, loop calls, accumulate returned IDs; add test for boundary. Also confirm empirical behavior of attachLeadsToCampaign on very large lead_ids arrays (QA F6 — not documented as capped, behavior unknown at scale).

items.title: Migrate signal-campaigns.ts from createLead to createOrUpdateLeadsMultiple
items.notes: QA F4 on commit 33a9c3c4. BL-088 fixed email-adapter.ts Step 4 but three call sites still use per-lead createLead POST: (a) src/lib/pipeline/signal-campaigns.ts:368-386 — signal pipeline deploys leads to workspace-scoped EB lead store; SECOND signal-campaign run will 422 on previously uploaded leads per the exact BL-088 failure mode. Current error handling at lines 380-385 catches per-lead errors and CONTINUES — so retained leads silently fail to attach, deploy "succeeds" with fewer leads than intended, no diagnostic surfaced. Operational risk on re-triggered signal campaigns. (b) src/lib/leads/operations.ts:821 — lead-export path. (c) src/mcp/leads-agent/tools/export.ts:192 — admin MCP tool with explicit error reporting (lowest urgency). Fix: (a) is the priority — swap to createOrUpdateLeadsMultiple with existing_lead_behavior=patch, match email-adapter pattern. (b) and (c) lower urgency but should be migrated for consistency. Dead-code cleanup of EmailBisonClient.createLead once all call sites migrated.

items.title: createOrUpdateLeadsMultiple: Step 4 batch 422 failure-mode test coverage
items.notes: QA F5 observational. Tolerant-parse drift tests in client-create-or-update-leads.test.ts assert the warn message contains regex markers (/\[BL-088\]/, /response drift/, /raw=/) but do not assert the warn actually captures the raw response body. If rawPreview generation fails (JSON.stringify on circular object → inner catch → "(unserializable)"), the warn still fires and regex still matches, but actual drift data is silently lost from ops log. Fix: add assertion that warnSpy.mock.calls[0][0] contains the stringified body from the mock (or "(empty body)" for empty case). Also: add a test for a BATCH 422 scenario (e.g. one malformed lead among 44) to document whether EB returns per-lead errors or fails the entire batch — not covered by current suite.

items.title: Comment rot: DEPLOY_STEP.ATTACH_LEADS table describes removed implementation

items.title: Admin pages show Unauthorized on expired session instead of redirecting to login
items.notes: User requested as BL-091; renumbered to BL-096 (BL-091 already taken). Priority: medium (UX, not data loss). Discovered 2026-04-16. Problem: When admin_session cookie expires (7-day TTL, src/lib/admin-auth.ts:11), GET /api/workspace/[slug]/members returns 401 via requireAdminAuth. UI (src/components/workspace/members-table.tsx:216-231) surfaces Unauthorized and renders "0 members · 0 active", looking like data is missing. Happens across ALL workspaces simultaneously because auth context is global. Root cause: no SSR auth gate on src/app/(admin)/layout.tsx — expired sessions render admin shell and only CSR fetches fail; user has no indication they need to re-auth. Fix options: (1 preferred) add requireAdminAuth in src/app/(admin)/layout.tsx -> redirect('/login') on null session — catches ALL admin pages, not just members; (2) MembersTable + siblings detect 401 and trigger re-login prompt. Scope: data-side confirmed clean — 28 members across 10 workspaces intact in Prisma. Pure UX/auth-flow bug. Not linked to BL-048/059/068 (verified via git log on admin-auth.ts — last auth-layer change pre-April, commit 87bec2a9). Queue for after Phase 6b canary + BL-074 push complete — do not interrupt current deploy rebuild work.

items.title: ooo-reengage.ts emits double-curly {{first_name}} — bypasses EB variable transformer, ships raw to recipients
items.notes: Source: monty-qa F1 review of commit 14bb69ba (BL-093 vendor-spec correction, 2026-04-17). trigger/ooo-reengage.ts lines 414/421/429 (writer prompt instructions) and line 461 (hardcoded fallback body) emit {{first_name}} and {{ooo_greeting}}. Variable transformer regex /(?<!\{)\{([A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*)\}(?!\})/g deliberately refuses double-curly — these pass through as literal text to EB. Active path: fires on every OOO reply received via Trigger.dev task. Not dormant. Fix: rewrite prompts + fallback to canonical single-curly {FIRSTNAME} (writer-side format) and let transformer map to {FIRST_NAME}. Verify vendor spec for {{ooo_greeting}} — may need custom EB variable or removal. Out of scope for the BL-093 correction commit per PM rule "do not touch writer prompts". Needs separate cycle.

items.title: {LOCATION} and {LASTEMAILMONTH} vendor decision — documented as RIGHT in writer-rules.md but transformer leaves them unmapped
items.notes: Source: monty-qa F3 review of commit 14bb69ba. writer-rules.md line 209 documents {LOCATION} and {LASTEMAILMONTH} as canonical. PM-provided vendor-authoritative EB spec (2026-04-17) does NOT include either. Transformer leaves them unmapped (pass through + BL-099 warn) — if any writer emits them, EB stores the literal token and recipients see raw text. Decision needed: (a) map to existing EB field (e.g. {LOCATION} -> {{notes}} like the prior wrong-spec attempt), (b) define workspace custom variables, or (c) remove from writer-rules.md RIGHT examples so writers stop emitting them. Latent, not active — current copy audit shows no producer emits either token today.

items.title: Sender-name transformer: short-body edge case rewrites mid-body salutation as signature
items.notes:
QA F1 on commit 41ba65cd. SIGNATURE_TAIL_LINE_COUNT=5 has no lower bound on body length — in a ≤5-line body the window covers the whole body, so a standalone sender-first-name line mid-body (e.g. a salutation addressing a prospect named Daniel) gets corrupted to {SENDER_FIRST_NAME}. Repro: roster={firstNames:['Daniel']...}, body='Hi there,
Daniel
How is the team?
Worth a chat?
Daniel Lazarus' → line 2 'Daniel' (recipient salutation) becomes '{SENDER_FIRST_NAME}'. Not affecting 1210-solutions canary today (6+ lines per step) but real risk for: (a) one-liner copy strategy (<70 word bodies, short), (b) multi-sender rosters where a prospect's first name happens to match any sender's first name. Gates wider rollout. Fix options per QA suggestion: (a) cap signature window to last 2-3 lines when total non-empty lines ≤ N; (b) require matched line to follow an empty line (own paragraph); (c) require absolute-last-3-positions regardless of body length. Add regression test using the concrete repro. File: src/lib/emailbison/sender-name-transform.ts:104,196-200.

items.title: Sender-name transformer cleanup: dead lastNames slot + untested buildSenderRoster + console.warn convention drift

Add src/lib/channels/__tests__/build-sender-roster.test.ts. (F4 LOW) sender-name-transform.ts:264 bare console.warn vs variable-transform.ts:153 inline eslint-disable comment — pick one convention and apply to both. no-console rule not active today, fold into BL-102 as stylistic touchup.

Built src/lib/emailbison/company-normaliser.ts (~270 LOC) + 24 vitest tests covering all 21 PM-specified edge cases. Applied at email-adapter.ts Step 4 ATTACH_LEADS callsite only — DB raw. items.title: LinkedIn: fix literal {UPPERCASE} variable tokens + missing company normalisation
items.notes: BlankTag LinkedIn sequences deliver literal {FIRSTNAME}/{COMPANYNAME} to prospects because writer emits {UPPERCASE} tokens but Handlebars renderer expects {{camelCase}}. Diagnostic at 19:25Z confirmed all 4 BlankTag LinkedIn campaigns affected. Also missing normalizeCompanyName at render boundary (8/156 leads have Ltd suffix). Fix: new src/lib/linkedin/variable-transform.ts + import existing normaliser at sequencing.ts. Sender-name hardcoding (separate bug #3) deferred — BlankTag has one LinkedIn sender, no immediate impact. RESOLVED: commit cb5f6673 — transform injected at compileTemplate() render-time before Handlebars.compile; normalizeCompanyName imported from emailbison namespace at buildTemplateContext person.company read. QA independently verified 4 real Ltd-suffix leads (Groomi Limited, Ledsone Uk Limited, Finnsøn LTD, Insulation4less Ltd) — zero {UPPERCASE} residue, zero suffix leaks. Token enumeration across all 8 active LinkedIn campaigns (1210 + Lime C1-C7) confirmed only {FIRSTNAME}/{COMPANYNAME}/{JOBTITLE}/{LASTEMAILMONTH} in use — all in VAR_MAP. Verdict: PASS_WITH_FINDINGS.

items.title: LinkedIn render boundary — follow-ups from BL-105 QA (F1/F2/F3)
items.notes: BL-105 QA (commit cb5f6673) surfaced three medium-severity quality gaps worth addressing before the next LinkedIn render-boundary change: (F1) {EMAIL} maps to {{email}} in VAR_MAP but buildTemplateContext does NOT bind email — silent empty-render at that position with no warn (asymmetric vs unknown tokens which do warn). Fix: drop {EMAIL} from VAR_MAP OR bind person.email in buildTemplateContext. (F2) jobTitle is not normalised at the render boundary. Lime C4 and C5 active campaigns use {JOBTITLE}. A lead with 'Warehouse Manager UK Ltd' in jobTitle would leak the suffix. Fix: apply a lighter suffix stripper to jobTitle — use LEGAL-only variant (not GEO, since 'UK Sales Director' etc. is legitimate). Consider extracting a stripLegalSuffixes function from BL-103's normaliser. (F3) No test asserts every VAR_MAP right-hand-side Handlebars key exists in buildTemplateContext's returned keys. The dev's JSDoc at variable-transform.ts:33-35 explicitly warns about the coupling hazard; F1 is a direct consequence. Fix: add a lockstep test that catches future desync automatically. Also ties in BL-104 F5 — normalizeCompanyName namespace collision (three variants: normalize.ts, normalizer/company.ts, emailbison/company-normaliser.ts, plus BL-105 added a fourth consumer). Consider moving BL-103's normaliser to src/lib/common/ once both channel adapters consume it. Ref: QA findings at .monty/memory/incidents.md entry [2026-04-16T19:17:00Z].

items.title: Apply company normaliser at THREE additional EB wire-call sites (BL-104 QA F1 HIGH)
items.notes: BL-104 QA F1 (commit a9e06317) surfaced three additional `EmailBisonClient.createLead` call sites that ship `person.company` raw to the wire, bypassing `normalizeCompanyName` entirely. BL-104 only patched `src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:879` (static-campaign deploy path via `createOrUpdateLeadsMultiple`). The three unpatched paths are: (1) `src/lib/leads/operations.ts:828` (`exportListToEmailBison`, used by Leads agent and `scripts/cli/list-export.ts` — any list-export to EB ships raw legal names); (2) `src/mcp/leads-agent/tools/export.ts:197` (custom campaign export with template duplication); (3) `src/lib/pipeline/signal-campaigns.ts:375` (signal campaign lead deployment pipeline, evergreen). Signal campaigns in particular run indefinitely and will ship 'Sonnic Support Solutions Ltd' to EB despite BL-104 landing. The canary (EB 92) went through email-adapter and is covered; future signal-campaign lead deployments on 1210-solutions or similar workspaces will not benefit until this is fixed. Fix: wrap each `company:` line with `normalizeCompanyName(person.company, person.companyDomain ?? null) ?? undefined`; add `companyDomain: true` to the corresponding Prisma `select` blocks (operations.ts:271, 537; signal-campaigns.ts:363). Add companion unit tests. Ref: incidents.md entry [2026-04-17T01:30:00Z].

items.title: Normaliser edge-case polish: trailing-comma bypass, warn dedup, protocol-prefix on domain (BL-104 QA F2/F3/F4)
items.notes: BL-104 QA found three non-blocking polish items against commit a9e06317: (F2 MEDIUM, src/lib/emailbison/company-normaliser.ts:462) `'Foo Ltd,'` (trailing comma, no whitespace) returns unchanged — entry-trim is `raw.trim()` (whitespace only); `trimTrailingPunct` only fires on a SUCCESSFUL strip, and the `\s+SUFFIX\.?$` regex anchors at `$` so the trailing comma blocks the match. DB audit today = 0 production rows affected, but CSV imports could reintroduce the shape. Fix: change entry-trim to `trimTrailingPunct(raw.trim())`; add tests for `'Foo Ltd,'`, `'Foo Ltd ,'`, `'Foo Inc ,  '`. (F3 MEDIUM, company-normaliser.ts:486) `console.warn` on MAX_ITERATIONS cap has zero deduplication — probe confirmed 1000 calls with same pathological input produce 1000 warn lines. Bulk 10K deploy with bad CSV would flood observability. Fix: module-level Set<string> or LRU to dedup by input hash OR rate-limit once-per-minute. Cross-check variable-transform.ts / sender-name-transform.ts for shared convention. (F4 LOW, company-normaliser.ts:379) `truncateByDomain` does not strip `http://` / `https://` protocol from the domain stem — `('Sonnic Support Solutions', 'https://sonnic.com')` returns unchanged because stem becomes `'https://sonnic'`. DB audit of 5000 rows = 0 affected today; silent-fail footgun for future enrichment paths. Fix: strip `https?://` before `www.` in domain-stem parser. Ref: incidents.md entry [2026-04-17T01:30:00Z].

items.title: 1210-solutions EB schedule timezone investigation + idempotent repair script
items.notes: PM audit on 2026-04-16 reported EB 94/95/96 (1210-solutions Construction/Industrial/Healthcare) had timezone=Europe/Dublin while EB 92 (Facilities/Cleaning canary) was Europe/London. Investigation via live `ebClient.getSchedule` on all four: ALL FOUR are Europe/London today (schedules id=66/67/68/69, created_at == updated_at means no manual edits). `DEFAULT_SCHEDULE` at src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:150 has been 'Europe/London' stably; both canary and remainder stage-deploy scripts run `executeDeploy(..., {skipResume:true})` which triggers Step 5 UPSERT_SCHEDULE (skipResume only gates Step 9/10). No drift exists. Most likely source of PM's Dublin reading: UI widget rendering schedule in the viewer's local browser timezone. Deliverables: `scripts/maintenance/_bl109-fetch-1210-schedules.ts` (read-only fetch) + `scripts/maintenance/_bl109-fix-timezones.ts` (idempotent guard/repair; dry-run default; preserves every other field via verbatim spread of GET response, overrides timezone only, strips EB-returned HH:MM:SS to HH:MM). Dry-run showed all 4 skip with `already correct` — zero PUTs performed. Scripts kept as a reusable repair path for any future drift of this class. DONE at commit f1744fb7.

items.title: Make EB schedule timezone workspace-configurable
items.notes: Discovered while investigating BL-109. `DEFAULT_SCHEDULE.timezone` at src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:150 is hardcoded 'Europe/London'. Unblocks future Ireland/EU/US client onboarding without another code release. Proposed change (scope-locked, additive): (1) add `scheduleTimezone String? @default("Europe/London")` column to `Workspace` in prisma/schema.prisma — nullable with a safe default, zero migration risk; (2) in src/lib/channels/email-adapter.ts:140-152, change DEFAULT_SCHEDULE into a function `buildSchedule(workspace)` that spreads the constant and overrides timezone from `workspace.scheduleTimezone ?? 'Europe/London'`; (3) at Step 5 call sites (email-adapter.ts:944/948/953/956/960/965), read `workspace.scheduleTimezone` from the Workspace row already loaded in `loadCampaignContext` and pass into `buildSchedule()`; (4) expose as a settings field on workspace admin UI (portal settings page). BLOCKED on: email-adapter.ts parallel-task ownership (br7jxqrhv chunking work in flight) — pick up AFTER that ships. Effort: ~1-2 hrs (schema + 1 code site + test update). No user-facing risk; defaults preserve current behaviour. Tests: extend src/__tests__/email-adapter-deploy.test.ts to verify `buildSchedule` returns workspace-overridden timezone when set, falls back to London otherwise.

items.title: Rollback EB-delete: harden conditional + clear CampaignDeploy.emailBisonCampaignId + add positive-path tests (rollback-fix QA F1/F2/F3)
items.notes: QA review of commit 8dd58ed1 (rollback EB-orphan-delete fix labelled 'BL-107' in commit msg — NAMING COLLISION with backlog BL-107 normaliser-callsites entry; this is a different code path) surfaced three findings against src/lib/campaigns/deploy.ts. (F1 HIGH) Pre-tx EB delete fires unconditionally even when the tx-gated DB rollback later short-circuits (e.g. admin pauses mid-deploy → Campaign.status moves off `deployed` → DB revert is skipped, but the EB campaign is already gone). Fix: gate the EB delete behind the same precondition that gates the DB rollback (read Campaign row first, only delete if status still indicates an in-flight deploy that this code owns). (F2 HIGH) CampaignDeploy.emailBisonCampaignId is preserved on rollback. After BL-107 deletes the EB draft, a subsequent Trigger.dev retry (BL-076) restores the dangling ebId on the Campaign row → Step 1's idempotency GET returns 404 → terminal 'manual delete?' error. Effectively eliminates maxAttempts=2 retry on any post-Step-1 failure. Fix: also null `CampaignDeploy.emailBisonCampaignId` inside the rollback transaction so the next retry takes the fresh-create branch. Dev flagged this in decisions.md but did not file a follow-up — this entry IS that follow-up. (F3 MEDIUM) Zero positive-path tests for the BL-107 EB delete: existing rollback tests in src/lib/campaigns/__tests__/deploy-rollback.test.ts silently fall into the pre-tx-snapshot catch because prismaMock.campaign.findUnique / .campaignDeploy.findFirst aren't stubbed — the new ebClient.deleteCampaign call is therefore not exercised by any test. Fix: add a positive-path test that stubs the snapshot reads, asserts ebClient.deleteCampaign was called with the right ID, and asserts AuditLog metadata contains ebOrphanDeleted=true. Ref: incidents.md entry [2026-04-17T03:50:00Z]. Until F1+F2 land, PM should prefer fresh re-approval over retryDeployChannel for any failed deploy that hit BL-107 rollback.
